128556.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag believes the municipality should always send understandable letters and emails to residents and entrepreneurs. The government must communicate clearly.

128557.explanation: The residents of The Hague elect the municipal council. That is the democratically elected representative body. In contrast, citizen councils selected by lot are not democratically elected. However, we support genuine resident participation, democratic citizen summits, and referenda on local issues.

128558.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag holds sport very close to its heart. We are in favor of investments in both elite and grassroots sports. Besides bringing a lot of joy to many people, sport is very good for The Hague's economy, entrepreneurs, and for promoting city pride.

128559.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag supports many more playgrounds for our children and, for example, more sports fields for our youth. In every neighborhood of The Hague, we think there should be more of these.

128560.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag is a warm advocate for more sports activities in The Hague. We strive to promote physical activity because sport not only brings people together but is also good for health.

128561.explanation: We support organizing major top-level sports events more often in The Hague. Besides providing great enjoyment for many people, these events are very good for the local economy, entrepreneurs, city pride, and city marketing. Of course, nuisance must be limited.

128562.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag believes the municipality should spend more money on promoting the city. We want to encourage quality tourism. For example, we think the Mauritshuis should be promoted worldwide. That is good for our Hague economy and city pride.

128563.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag wants The Hague to become the congress capital of the Netherlands. The city must invest in the World Forum. Conferences provide a great deal of revenue for The Hague's economy. Of course, nuisance must be minimized.

128564.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag does not understand why the municipality bans advertisements for completely legal products like air travel. We find this outright patronizing. We would like to reverse this ban.

128565.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag wants permits for entrepreneurs to be issued more quickly. Entrepreneurs are the backbone of the economy and the job engine of our city. The municipality should not hinder but facilitate entrepreneurs.

128566.explanation: Fishing is inextricably linked to Scheveningen, and we should be proud of that. It would be good if the municipality of The Hague would express that pride more and more often, for example by serving delicious North Sea fish at gatherings.

128567.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag supports a smaller and more efficient government. As far as we are concerned, the number of policy civil servants should decrease, and the number of executing civil servants, such as enforcement officers and garbage collectors, should increase. We also want to strengthen the Urban Development Service.

128568.explanation: We do not want to accommodate any more asylum seekers in our city. The so-called absorption capacity of The Hague has been exceeded because we have shortages in everything: housing, police officers, teachers, and healthcare personnel. Hart voor Den Haag makes an exception for Ukrainian women and children.

128569.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag naturally supports dealing with energy as wisely as possible. However, we do not see it as the municipality's task to monitor companies on this. The regulatory burden on entrepreneurs should be reduced.

128570.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag welcomes a 24-hour economy in economic core and opportunity areas. Nuisance must, of course, be kept to a minimum.

128571.explanation: It goes without saying that every rental home should be sufficiently insulated. Tenants have a right to this. However, considering the enormous shortage of rental homes in The Hague, it is too far for us to prohibit landlords from renting out a home that is not yet sufficiently insulated.

128572.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag wants the beach to be accessible to everyone, including people with disabilities. We therefore advocate more ramps, although we do not think they need to be at every beach entrance.

128573.explanation: Large parts of The Hague suffer from a massive waste problem. We support placing more underground waste containers, preferably not at the expense of parking spaces and greenery. We also find it important that there is local support for this.

128574.explanation: We would like to see water flowing again in as many previously filled-in canals as possible. The Hague could be a beautiful canal city by the sea. Canals look nice, serve for water storage, promote recreation and quality tourism, and are therefore good for The Hague's economy.

128575.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag wants to reverse all zero-emission zones in the city, both in the city center and along the coast. These zones are bad for the economy and bad for entrepreneurs. Before you know it, you won't find a plumber or painter in large parts of the city anymore.

128576.explanation: The Hague is struggling with seagull nuisance. The nuisance must be drastically reduced, with proper attention to animal welfare, of course.

128577.explanation: Unfortunately, not every young person has a nice place at home to study. That is why we support sufficient study places in, for example, libraries. As far as we are concerned, they should also be accessible in the evenings and at weekends.

128578.explanation: The Hague is becoming more and more a student city. That naturally calls for sufficient student housing. We would like to concentrate that on a campus boulevard in the city center and near the major stations.

128579.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag wants the Compulsory Education Act to be properly enforced. Every child has the right to education.

128580.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag holds art and culture close to its heart. More budget may certainly be allocated for this. Art and culture strengthen social cohesion and give an impulse to the city and The Hague’s economy.

128581.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag advocates for more libraries. Libraries are the beating heart of the neighborhood and promote knowledge, language, social interaction, and opportunities.

128582.explanation: Safety is the government's core task. Everyone should always and everywhere feel safe. We want more cameras, more police officers, and to double the number of enforcement officers. We established a ‘Unsafe Places Reporting Point.’ Street lighting and pruning work are also important in this context.

128583.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag wants to close the Doubletstraat as a prostitution area. We want to keep street prostitution controlled and manageable. Nuisance should be limited as much as possible.

128584.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag stands firmly behind the bonfires in Duindorp and Scheveningen. We cherish this beautiful tradition. It goes without saying that the bonfires must be held safely.

128585.explanation: Nuisance in Scheveningen must be tackled firmly. Police officers and riot police are armed. Hart voor Den Haag also supports enforcement officers equipped with batons and/or pepper spray, but they must be properly trained. Enforcement must be done with common sense.

128586.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag opposes an overconcentration of coffee shops in certain neighborhoods. That does not benefit livability in those areas. We oppose a coffee shop in De Uithof.

128587.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag is not opposed to more hotels in The Hague. Quality tourism benefits our city’s economy. Of course, nuisance must be limited. Furthermore, we believe hotels are for tourists and not for accommodating, for example, asylum seekers.

128588.explanation: The tax burden in the Netherlands is far too high. We want to lower the property tax (ozb). That is good for your wallet.

128589.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag believes that non-status holders should not have priority for social rental housing; instead, residents of The Hague, Scheveningers, Duindorp residents, and Loosduiners should. We believe this should also apply to new-build homes. Residents of The Hague first.

128590.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag wants to prevent vacancies as much as possible. High fines should be imposed for long-term vacancies, and the municipality should apply a vacancy regulation.

128591.explanation: Hart voor Den Haag believes residents of The Hague should have priority for social rental housing. This also applies to young people from The Hague living at home out of necessity or to residents of The Hague who are divorced.

128592.explanation: The Hague is facing a desperate shortage of teachers, police officers, and healthcare personnel. Hart voor Den Haag wants to roll out the red carpet for these heroes. Therefore, they should also receive priority for social rental housing.

128593.explanation: We want people to be able to continue living in their own neighborhood and district. That is why we believe, for example, Scheveningers should have priority for social rental or new-build housing in Scheveningen. The same applies to, among others, Duindorp residents in Duindorp and Loosduiners in Loosduinen.

128594.explanation: Large parts of The Hague suffer from nuisance caused by an overconcentration of labor migrants. Illegal (room) housing must be tackled harshly. The Hague Property Brigade should focus on combating overcrowding.

128595.explanation: Heart for The Hague firmly supports the victims of the Benefits Scandal. What these people have suffered at the hands of the government is indescribable. Victims must receive what they are entitled to. The municipality of The Hague must hold the national government accountable for this.

128596.explanation: Heart for The Hague is compassionate towards those who cannot help themselves and tough on those who do not want to. Fraud must always be tackled rigorously, including fraud in social assistance.

128597.explanation: Heart for The Hague has great sympathy for cyclists. We support wide, well-maintained bike paths, more bicycle racks, and more (underground) guarded bike parking facilities.

128598.explanation: If there is support in the neighborhood or district, Heart for The Hague favors a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h in residential areas. On through roads, Heart for The Hague supports a maximum speed limit of (at least) 50 km/h. This is also important for the smooth passage of emergency services.

128599.explanation: There is a huge shortage of parking spaces in large parts of The Hague. Therefore, we want more parking spaces, preferably underground. We also support a much greener city with many more trees. More parking spaces and more greenery do not have to be mutually exclusive.

128600.explanation: Heart for The Hague supports the tunneling of various tram lines, as this improves accessibility in The Hague. We also want, for example, RandstadRail 2 to be extended to Vroondaal and RandstadRail 3 to Kijkduin, preferably underground.

128601.explanation: Heart for The Hague believes that all children between 4 and 12 years old should be able to use trams and buses for free. We also want to investigate the possibility of free public transport for people aged 65 and over.

128602.explanation: Some streets in The Hague resemble race tracks. In those streets, Heart for The Hague strongly supports the installation of noise speed cameras.

128603.explanation: Heart for The Hague only supports paid parking if there is support for it in the neighborhood due to high parking pressure.

128604.explanation: A personal budget (pgb) is funded by taxpayers. Therefore, a pgb should only be granted if it is necessary.

128605.explanation: The waiting lists in youth care are alarming. They must be drastically reduced, partly by providing sufficient resources and partly by carefully assessing who should or should not qualify for youth care.

128606.explanation: Smoking and vaping are very harmful to health. Children and young people must be especially protected against this. Therefore, we support a smoking and vaping ban around schools and playgrounds.

128607.explanation: Unfortunately, many people in The Hague struggle with problematic debts. Therefore, Heart for The Hague considers it very important to educate children in schools about responsible money management. Early intervention prevents further debt and evictions.

128608.explanation: Every resident of The Hague who needs home care has the right to it, without unnecessary hassle or complications. However, abuse of home care and other services must be tackled firmly.

128609.explanation: As long as the municipality's financial situation permits, we oppose personal contributions for the use of school transportation and care taxis.

128610.explanation: Heart for The Hague has always advocated for keeping Bronovo hospital open. It is incomprehensible that hospitals are closing in a rapidly aging city. Bronovo must remain open at minimum as a hospital with emergency services and outpatient clinics. There should also be a large general practitioner post at Bronovo.

128611.explanation: Nuisance caused by an overconcentration of labor migrants in certain districts of The Hague must be ended. Heart for The Hague believes that labor migrants working in the Westland municipality should preferably also live in that municipality.

128612.explanation: Heart for The Hague wants to plant at least 3,000 trees net in The Hague in the coming council term (2026-2030). The Hague needs to become much greener.

128613.explanation: Heart for The Hague wants approximately 30 percent of newly built homes to be social rental housing. Any figure above that is unfeasible and completely unrealistic. Moreover, we want to strengthen neighborhoods by adding strong shoulders.

128614.explanation: Heart for The Hague stands fully behind our anglers.

128615.explanation: There is a gigantic shortage of housing in our city. The shortage is so severe that creative solutions must be found. Heart for The Hague is not unfavourable to land purchases outside the municipality in this context. However, we are fundamentally against building in green areas.

128616.explanation: Heart for The Hague attaches great importance to youth work. There should be no budget cuts in this area.

128556.explanation: It is important that residents can clearly understand what the municipality communicates. Therefore, the text in letters and emails must be clear to everyone.

128557.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers it important that residents can participate in decisions about the city. A citizens' assembly can help with this. We ensure that everyone can join and that residents have influence over budget spending in their neighborhoods via neighborhood agendas.

128558.explanation: Top-level sports (events) can be inspiring, but the municipality's money and attention should primarily benefit residents through amateur sports. Funding should only go to top sports if it demonstrably contributes to sports clubs, inclusion, and physical activity for residents.

128559.explanation: Playing outside is fun and beneficial for development. Every child has the right to a safe and pleasant playground close to home.

128560.explanation: Everyone should be able to exercise and be active safely, regardless of age, income, or disability. We will continue to support queer sports spaces as well. Together, this helps ensure a healthy and active The Hague.

128561.explanation: Top-level sports (events) can be inspiring, but the municipality's money and attention should primarily benefit residents through amateur sports. Funding should only go to top sports if it demonstrably contributes to sports clubs, inclusion, and physical activity for residents.

128562.explanation: The Hague is not a commercial product for display, but a city that revolves around its residents. Money for city promotion can be better spent on culture and events. These make the city attractive and naturally attract visitors.

128563.explanation: Large conferences belong to an international city like The Hague. GroenLinks-PvdA believes it is unnecessary for the municipality to spend money on this. The money can be better used for events in which the entire city can participate.

128564.explanation: In The Hague, there is no room for advertising polluting practices like air travel holidays and diesel cars. GroenLinks-PvdA fully supports the ban on fossil fuel advertising.

128565.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to prevent unnecessary waiting times for entrepreneurs. It remains important to carefully check compliance with conditions so that the interests of other residents, nature, and the environment are also adequately considered.

128566.explanation: We do not see promoting the fishing industry in Scheveningen as a core task of the municipality.

128567.explanation: Sufficient municipal employees are needed to implement city plans. This enables the municipality to assist residents well and provide good services.

128568.explanation: People fleeing war and violence and political refugees have the right to shelter, and we must arrange this as well as possible. All municipalities must contribute. Hosting fewer asylum seekers means other municipalities have to do more, which is unfair.

128569.explanation: Just like residents, we ask companies to take responsibility in the climate transition. Ultimately, this is a win-win: you save not only CO2 but also energy costs. GroenLinks-PvdA wants stricter inspections, with extra focus on major energy consumers.

128570.explanation: Casinos and gambling halls do not deserve a special status. This prevents people at risk of gambling addiction from becoming even more vulnerable to addiction and financial problems.

128571.explanation: Every resident of The Hague has the right to a good home. Poor insulation leads to cold homes and higher energy bills. We expect both housing corporations and private landlords to improve their homes.

128572.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants a city accessible to everyone. People using wheelchairs should also have access to our beautiful coast.

128573.explanation: Underground containers reduce litter and pests on the streets. This helps prevent littering and offers everyone a clean and livable neighborhood.

128574.explanation: Opening canals makes the city more beautiful, pleasant, and cooler in summer. The Piet Heinplein shows that this works. However, these are expensive projects, so the money must be distributed fairly across the city.

128575.explanation: Polluting commercial and freight vehicles do not belong in the city center or along the coast. With clean vehicles, we protect nature, create space for housing development (nitrogen space), and keep the city healthy for people with lung problems.

128576.explanation: Relocating seagull nests is not animal-friendly and we do not support it. Nuisance can be addressed differently, for example by making roofs less suitable and by keeping streets clean.

128577.explanation: Not every child or student has the luxury of their own room or a quiet place to study. By extending the library opening hours, we give everyone the chance to study peacefully.

128578.explanation: We ensure more affordable housing for young people and students. Where possible, with shared spaces, such as in student houses. This way, young people find not only affordable but also pleasant housing. We make it easier to live together with multiple friends.

128579.explanation: Every child has the right to education. We do not want any children sitting at home and we tackle waiting lists. Together with schools, welfare, and youth care, we assess per neighborhood what is needed to provide every child with a place.

128580.explanation: We want more funding for arts and culture. Spending should grow in line with the population. This way, The Hague can eventually spend as much per capita on culture as other major cities.

128581.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants a permanent location for the library in Mariahoeve. In addition, we support extended opening hours and free library membership for everyone.

128582.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants The Hague to become the safest city for women and girls. Besides lighting and trimming vegetation, it is necessary that women have a say in how the city is designed.

128583.explanation: A phase-out policy for window prostitution is harmful to the safety and well-being of sex workers. In The Hague, there remains a place for sex workers who prefer window prostitution. We work on improving livability in and around the window prostitution streets.

128584.explanation: As long as the bonfires are organized safely and according to rules and agreements (for example, cleaning the beach), the municipality can continue to allow the bonfires.

128585.explanation: Batons and pepper spray do not lead to increased safety but rather have a greater chance of escalating conflicts.

128586.explanation: It's not about having fewer coffee shops but about a better distribution across the city. In some neighborhoods, such as Zeeheldenkwartier and Weimarstraat, there should be fewer. Additionally, together with operators and residents, we address livability around coffee shops.

128587.explanation: We choose housing, not hotel rooms. The Hague cannot afford to lose our scarce space for housing to hotel rooms, various forms of Airbnb, and short stays.

128588.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in The Hague is currently very low. Meanwhile, our city faces major challenges: housing construction, sustainability, keeping the city accessible. We must invest in these areas. That is why we support raising the property tax to the average level of the four major cities.

128589.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA maintains priority for status holders (besides other vulnerable housing seekers, such as homeless people). This enables them to quickly participate in society.

128590.explanation: Vacancy during times of housing shortage is unacceptable. Thanks to GroenLinks-PvdA, The Hague will finally have a vacancy ordinance. We will enforce it strictly and fine for vacancy. We will also introduce the vacancy tax, which GroenLinks-PvdA secured in the House of Representatives, in The Hague.

128591.explanation: We make sure that enough vacated homes are reserved for homeless people and youth from youth care. Thanks to GroenLinks-PvdA, The Hague is already working on doubling this type of housing (covenant homes).

128592.explanation: High rents drive teachers and nurses out of the city. That is unacceptable. The real solution to the housing shortage is more social rental housing for everyone. GroenLinks-PvdA wants half of new construction to be social rental and invests 150 million euros in affordable housing.

128593.explanation: More building takes place in some neighborhoods than others. Always giving priority to residents in one neighborhood is unfair to residents of neighborhoods with fewer housing opportunities. The real solution to the housing shortage is more social rental housing for everyone. We want to invest heavily in this.

128594.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports stricter control of misconduct by landlords. However, the interests of tenants must always come first. We want to prevent tenants from losing their homes due to overly strict inspections.

128595.explanation: Victims of the childcare benefits scandal deserve all the support the municipality can offer.

128596.explanation: Large-scale efforts to combat fraud lead to the criminalization of people on social assistance and to scandals like the childcare benefits scandal. It helps no one.

128597.explanation: We want more people to use bicycles. Therefore, there must be enough safe bike racks and parking facilities. This keeps sidewalks and streets neat and accessible, also for people in wheelchairs.

128598.explanation: The Hague should be a city where children can play safely on the streets and where cyclists and pedestrians have priority. A lower maximum speed is one of the most effective measures to achieve zero traffic fatalities.

128599.explanation: More greenery in the city makes neighborhoods nicer places to live. By replacing parking spaces with green areas and parks, there is more space to play, meet, and relax. This is good for the livability of The Hague.

128600.explanation: Sufficient (and affordable) public transport is essential for an accessible and reachable city. Therefore, we want enough tram lines, for example to Binckhorst, Southwest, and Kijkduin.

128601.explanation: Free tram and bus travel for children up to 12 years old is important. This allows all children to travel easily through the city, even if their parents have little money. We want this to remain and to extend it to children up to 16 years old.

128602.explanation: We will combat traffic nuisance caused by noisy cars and motorcycles. More electric transport and more space for bicycles create quieter traffic.

128603.explanation: Parking space is limited. We introduce paid parking to ensure that residents can still park their own cars. To prevent vans without permits and abandoned cars from continuously moving around, we will gradually implement paid parking throughout the entire city.

128604.explanation: A personal budget gives people the option to decide what care they purchase. It can also be a solution for informal caregivers. GroenLinks-PvdA believes in freedom of choice; people know best what they need.

128605.explanation: Children who need youth care must receive help quickly. We closely monitor waiting times and ensure children get urgent care immediately. Parents and children participate in deciding on appropriate support.

128606.explanation: Seeing smoking makes smoking more attractive. By banning smoking and vaping near playgrounds and schools, we protect children from addiction. We expand smoke- and vape-free zones and allow fewer new tobacco shops.

128607.explanation: Tackling debt means solving problems and preventing them. We help people in time with financial worries and make it easier to talk about money. Where necessary, the municipality takes over debts so people quickly regain peace of mind.

128608.explanation: The municipality should trust people who apply for household help. They know best what help is needed. By granting help more easily, people live more comfortably and we reduce pressure on informal caregivers.

128609.explanation: If you need care or support, you should not have to pay an extra penalty for it. In The Hague, we offer help to those who need it.

128610.explanation: We see many opportunities to preserve the Bronovo building for the neighborhood and city, including a healthcare function and possibly senior housing as well. Unfortunately, maintaining Bronovo as a full hospital is no longer possible.

128611.explanation: Migrant workers deserve the same rental rights and protections as any other tenant. Therefore, The Hague supports a quick separation of housing and employment, so that migrant workers are less vulnerable to exploitation and homelessness. Sectors that mistreat migrant workers must shrink.

128612.explanation: Trees are important for a green and pleasant city. They provide shade, clean air, and nature nearby. If a tree is cut down, it must be compensated with new trees. Preferably, we avoid cutting down trees.

128613.explanation: Everyone has the right to a roof over their head. The waiting lists for social rental housing are enormous. To ensure everyone can live comfortably in our city, we must build at least 50% social rental housing in the coming years. We invest 150 million euros in this.

128614.explanation: The Hague is a busy city where water is primarily intended for nature, recreation, and relaxation. Angling fits less and less and causes disturbance to animals and the environment. Therefore, we support a ban.

128615.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to tackle the housing shortage by building within the existing city. New construction is largely concentrated in the Southwest, around the stations, and in the Binckhorst. But if there are opportunities elsewhere, we will also seize them. Nature reserves must not be built on.

128616.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants more youth workers on the streets, in schools, and online. We choose prevention, perspective, and trust. For older youth, we will deploy more (and structural) street coaches.

128556.explanation: In The Hague, everyone should be able to participate. That means the municipality uses understandable language, also for people with limited reading or writing skills. D66 believes the municipality should set a good example in this.

128557.explanation: D66 has strongly advocated for citizen assemblies in all neighborhoods. This way, residents can participate in important decisions. Citizen assemblies strengthen trust in democracy and ensure policies better reflect what is happening in the city.

128558.explanation: D66 invests in sports for everyone-including top sports. Top sports inspire, connect, and contribute to The Hague’s economy, as long as they go hand in hand with strong sports clubs for young and old.

128559.explanation: Playing outside is important for health and development. D66 wants more and better playgrounds, especially in neighborhoods where children have less space. This ensures equal opportunities to play and exercise safely.

128560.explanation: Sports promote health, social interaction, and mental resilience. D66 wants to make sports accessible with special attention to youth, seniors, and people with disabilities, so everyone can participate.

128561.explanation: Appropriate top sports events enhance The Hague’s international profile, generate economic impact, and encourage residents to be more active-provided they are well organized and fit the city.

128562.explanation: D66 chooses promotion that contributes to a strong economy, culture, and livability. Not mass tourism, but targeted promotion that advances The Hague.

128563.explanation: The Hague is the international city of peace and justice. International conferences and meetings strengthen our economy, create jobs, and make the city attractive for entrepreneurs and knowledge institutions.

128564.explanation: This is already prohibited, and D66 wants to keep it that way. The Hague focuses on sustainable ambitions. That does not mean banning everything, but the municipality will not give extra platforms to polluting products in public spaces.

128565.explanation: Entrepreneurs keep the city running. D66 wants fast, clear, and predictable permitting through a single desk, so entrepreneurs know what to expect and have room to operate. That is good for the economy and employment.

128566.explanation: Scheveningen is very important for The Hague’s economy. D66 supports promoting Scheveningen and advocates for sustainable fisheries. A requirement to serve specific North Sea fish at events is not needed, and there must also be work on vegetarian/vegan options.

128567.explanation: D66 does not aim to reduce the number of municipal employees as a goal in itself but wants good service and a municipality close to its residents. D66 is committed to organizing this as efficiently as possible.

128568.explanation: D66 stands for humane reception and good distribution across the Netherlands. The Hague must take its responsibility and keep agreements but may simultaneously advocate at the national government that The Hague would like a lower task due to the housing shortage.

128569.explanation: Energy saving is necessary for the climate and affordability. D66 combines clear rules with support to achieve these goals.

128570.explanation: D66 wants to limit gambling addiction and nuisance. Clear opening hours can help protect vulnerable people and improve neighborhood livability.

128571.explanation: Tenants have the right to a healthy and affordable home. D66 wants landlords to invest in insulation, so tenants have lower energy costs and homes are future-proof.

128572.explanation: The city and the beach are for everyone. D66 wants beach access points to be accessible to people with disabilities, so everyone can enjoy the Hague coast.

128573.explanation: A clean city is a livable city. D66 wants more underground containers where possible, combined with better collection. This reduces nuisance from torn-open garbage bags.

128574.explanation: Reopening canals improves livability, provides cooling, and strengthens the city’s character. We look at where this is possible, with input from residents.

128575.explanation: D66 chooses clean air and healthy neighborhoods. The environmental zone remains in the city center and along the coast. This benefits residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs and provides clear rules.

128576.explanation: D66 carefully balances animal welfare and livability. Nuisance should preferably be addressed by other means. Relocating nests is not a standard measure and can only be done as a last resort.

128577.explanation: Not everyone has a good study place at home. D66 wants libraries and study places to be open more often in the evenings and weekends to reduce inequality and make learning possible for everyone.

128578.explanation: The Hague is a vibrant student city. Students have the right to good housing. D66 wants active construction of more student accommodation.

128579.explanation: D66 has worked hard on this during the last council term. We are working toward zero out-of-school youths, with rapid support, good cooperation, and education suited to every child.

128580.explanation: Art and culture enrich the city, connect people, and strengthen neighborhoods. D66 wants to invest in art and culture for everyone.

128581.explanation: Libraries are crucial for learning, reading, and equal opportunities. D66 wants to open new libraries in neighborhoods that currently lack them and strengthen existing libraries as places for learning and meeting.

128582.explanation: Everyone must feel safe on the street, especially women and young people. D66 invests in good lighting, clear streets, and maintenance of greenery. This increases social safety and ensures everyone can move freely in the city.

128583.explanation: D66 sees alternatives to window prostitution. But the safety and position of sex workers come first. Therefore, changes to window prostitution can only be made in consultation with the sex workers themselves and if the alternatives are safe and fully adequate.

128584.explanation: Traditions are important, but safety and health come first. D66 wants bonfires to be allowed only if they are safe, manageable, and responsible for residents, emergency services, and the environment.

128585.explanation: D66 chooses safety on the street but critically assesses armament. The principle is de-escalation, good training, and cooperation with the police. Additional resources are only considered if demonstrably necessary and proportionate.

128586.explanation: D66 wants to reduce nuisance and opts for a fair distribution of coffeeshops across the city. Not the number is central, but livability, safety, and clear enforcement.

128587.explanation: The Hague is an international city. D66 is open to moderate growth of hotels, fitting the city and without mass tourism. Hotels contribute to employment, conferences, and a strong local economy.

128588.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in The Hague is lower than in other major cities. D66 wants to gradually increase it to the average so we can invest in better facilities, good public transport, schools, parks, and strong economic development of the city.

128589.explanation: Status holders have the right, like everyone, to a good start in society. At the same time, D66 continues to focus on more housing and better flow for everyone.

128590.explanation: Homes are made for living in. D66 actively tackles vacancy, including fines for long-term vacancy, to alleviate the housing shortage and keep neighborhoods livable.

128591.explanation: Homelessness is a housing problem. D66 chooses 'Housing First': people who are homeless get their own home as quickly as possible, with appropriate guidance where needed.

128592.explanation: Teachers, nurses, and police officers are indispensable for The Hague. D66 finds it logical to help them find affordable housing so they can come to work in the city in the professions where shortages exist.

128593.explanation: D66 understands the desire for connection to the neighborhood but also wants a fair and transparent housing allocation. Housing policy must contribute to mixed and livable neighborhoods.

128594.explanation: D66 is in favor of stricter action against illegal room occupancy. This is necessary to combat nuisance, exploitation, and unsafe living conditions, and to protect the livability of neighborhoods. At the same time, we must invest in more student housing.

128595.explanation: The municipality must stand alongside the victims. D66 chooses recovery, customized solutions, and trust, so that people can rebuild their lives.

128596.explanation: D66 assumes trust in residents. Fraud must be strictly monitored, but without unnecessarily distrusting people or trapping them in bureaucracy.

128597.explanation: The bicycle is the main means of transport in the city. D66 invests in extra bike racks at stores, schools, and public transport stops to make cycling more attractive and reduce nuisance from incorrectly parked bikes.

128598.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount. D66 wants to implement 30 km/h speed limits in residential areas as soon as possible to prevent accidents and make streets safer and more livable.

128599.explanation: D66 wants to return the street to residents in places where there are actually too many parking spaces. Fewer parking spots and more greenery provide cooling, opportunities for social interaction, and a more pleasant living environment.

128600.explanation: Good public transport is essential for a growing city. D66 invests in new tram lines and better connections to keep neighborhoods accessible and livable.

128601.explanation: D66 wants to maintain this. Free public transport for children makes the city more accessible, encourages sustainable travel, and helps families.

128602.explanation: D66 wants stricter enforcement against traffic noise, for example with noise measurements, to make living in the city more pleasant.

128603.explanation: D66 believes residents should have a say in their neighborhood. Therefore, they should be able to contribute ideas about the introduction of paid parking, for example through support surveys.

128604.explanation: People who need care should not get lost in rules. D66 wants less bureaucracy, more trust, and faster allocation of personal budgets (pgb).

128605.explanation: D66 spares no effort to reduce waiting times. Children and families must receive timely appropriate help without unnecessary referrals or bureaucracy.

128606.explanation: D66 works toward a smoke-free generation. Children deserve a healthy environment, free from smoking and vaping near schools, playgrounds, and sports clubs.

128607.explanation: Debts must be addressed quickly. D66 wants low-threshold and accessible debt assistance, based on trust and with minimal bureaucracy.

128608.explanation: D66 assumes trust but wants to talk with people receiving home care. Together, we will assess whether cleaning help is really necessary. This way, we preserve enough care for those who need it most.

128609.explanation: D66 wants care and education to remain accessible. Additional costs must not be an obstacle for people dependent on transportation.

128610.explanation: D66 supports concentrating complex care where it improves quality. Not every hospital needs all departments, but good acute and emergency care must be timely accessible for everyone, everywhere in The Hague.

128611.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for good housing for seasonal workers. At the same time, D66 wants that workers who lose their jobs do not immediately lose their housing, to prevent exploitation.

128612.explanation: Greenery is essential for health and livability. D66 wants at least two trees replanted for every one cut down and additional greening in neighborhoods with few shaded spots, to provide cooling in summer.

128613.explanation: D66 chooses mixed neighborhoods. There must be more social rental housing and also more affordable owner-occupied homes. A citywide obligation of 50% social rental housing would slow down the construction of other types of homes that are also urgently needed for housing market flow.

128614.explanation: D66 sees recreational fishing as a small-scale activity that can continue with respect for nature and animals. A total ban is not necessary.

128615.explanation: The housing shortage is large. D66 wants to build many additional homes: within the city and, where necessary, also beyond the current city limits. This way, there will be space for greenery and amenities in the city, keeping The Hague livable.

128616.explanation: Youth work prevents problems, offers perspective, and strengthens neighborhoods. D66 invests specifically in youth work to increase opportunities and prevent problems.

128556.explanation: The municipality communicates in clear language. This ensures every resident of The Hague understands what is written and what to expect.

128557.explanation: A citizens' assembly is not elected and does not account to the city. The Hague VVD prefers to ensure a strong role for the municipal council, as democratically elected representatives of all residents, and good participation where the voices of residents are genuinely heard.

128558.explanation: Sports bring people together, provide enjoyment and confidence. We want to invest in sports for everyone. We want to fully accommodate top sports but not subsidize them. Naturally, top sports events, such as the Hockey World Cup, are welcome in The Hague to inspire children for sports.

128559.explanation: Many neighborhoods in The Hague currently have too few play areas. We want to add 35 new play areas in the city each year to ensure sufficient space for children to play and meet national standards.

128560.explanation: Sports bring people together, provide enjoyment and confidence. We want to invest in sports for everyone. More running routes and fitness spots will be created in green areas and at the beach.

128561.explanation: We want to fully accommodate top sports but not subsidize them. We want to attract top sports events, such as the Hockey World Cup, a major sailing race, equestrian sports, or the Beach Volleyball World Tour to The Hague. This can also inspire children to take up sports.

128562.explanation: With our beaches, museums, attractions, and beautiful historic city center, The Hague is the most enjoyable destination in the Netherlands. We want to promote The Hague more to attract high-quality tourism, benefiting museums, hospitality, and attractions, without the nuisance of mass tourism.

128563.explanation: Conferences are important for the economy because conference attendees spend much more money in The Hague than regular tourists. Conferences therefore create jobs in The Hague. This should not be cut but invested in.

128564.explanation: The ban on advertisements for flying and cars is pure nannying that should be abolished as soon as possible. The Hague VVD wants people to choose for themselves how they travel and go on holiday and is against this kind of censorship.

128565.explanation: Services to entrepreneurs must improve significantly. We want a single counter for entrepreneurs and events, offering assistance with questions, permits, and debt counseling. We want less paperwork and that civil servants work with entrepreneurs to find what is possible instead of only saying what is not allowed.

128566.explanation: We fully support the Scheveningen fishing industry. Our fishery is part of Scheveningen, like the lighthouse and the flag with three crowned herrings. Fishing is economically important and contributes to food security. The municipality sets a good example by purchasing North Sea fish for events.

128567.explanation: In the past five years, nearly 20% more civil servants have been hired. We want the municipality to focus on core tasks instead of nannying, like bans on advertisements for flying holidays. However, we do strengthen departments responsible for a clean and safe city, housing construction, and permits.

128568.explanation: The national government requires The Hague to accommodate over 2,000 asylum seekers. This is not feasible. The Hague is the most densely populated city in the Netherlands and has many other groups needing shelter, such as the homeless. Therefore, we want to revise the asylum seeker quota, aiming for a halving.

128569.explanation: We want enforcement focused on major issues instead of obstructing well-meaning entrepreneurs. Think of enforcement against shady businesses or the deterioration of industrial areas. Regarding sustainability, we want to support entrepreneurs.

128570.explanation: Many casinos already close part of the night. Some are open 24 hours a day. Online gambling is available 24/7 anyway. If there is nuisance, The Hague VVD wants to take measures.

128571.explanation: We focus on quickly improving housing rather than banning rentals during housing shortages. Therefore, we want to strengthen The Hague's Housing Enforcement Team. They ensure landlords rent out homes that are clean, intact, and safe.

128572.explanation: We want public spaces to be as accessible as possible to everyone. The beach makes The Hague unique, and everyone deserves to be able to go there. As long as not all beach access points are accessible, we want clear information online and on signage about which beach accesses are accessible.

128573.explanation: In the fight against litter and seagull nuisance, we must move away from garbage bags on the street. Neighborhoods that still have garbage bags on the street will receive underground containers. If that is truly not possible, customized solutions will be provided, such as an above-ground container.

128574.explanation: Canals are good for managing heavy rainfall, bring cooling during hot summers, and are fun to boat or fish on. That is why we, for example, reopened the canal at Piet Heinplein and will bring back the Hofgracht near the Binnenhof. Wherever we can reintroduce more water, we will do so.

128575.explanation: Clean air is important for our health. Therefore, we want to gradually work towards fewer emissions. We choose a realistic pace. Affordable electric trucks must be available and there must be sufficient electricity. This is not yet the case. Hence, more time is needed.

128576.explanation: Seagulls belong to The Hague. But they currently cause nuisance because they breed on roofs instead of in the dunes. Therefore, we will apply for a permit to remove nests where necessary. We use drones to specifically locate nests and intervene carefully.

128577.explanation: Every pupil and student in The Hague should have a quiet place to do homework, for example at their own school or in a nearby library. We want libraries to be open more often outside office hours.

128578.explanation: There is currently a large shortage of rooms for students. Therefore, we want more space for student housing, for example in the city center. Renting out a room should also be made easier according to The Hague VVD. Homeowners with space may rent out one room without a permit.

128579.explanation: The number of truants must be reduced to zero. Good education gives children equal opportunities. They learn knowledge and skills, values and norms. The municipality supports schools to keep students in education.

128580.explanation: Culture can make the city more enjoyable and attractive for residents and visitors. The Hague VVD wants more art in the streets that makes The Hague even more worth visiting. In the coming years, investment is also needed in the Berlage building of the Kunstmuseum, which is almost 100 years old.

128581.explanation: The Hague must keep libraries accessible for all residents. We want appropriate offerings per neighborhood and longer opening hours so you can also visit the library in the evenings or on Sundays.

128582.explanation: We expect everyone living in The Hague to treat women equally and with respect. On the streets, we ensure safe routes with good lighting and camera surveillance where necessary. We ask women to report unsafe places and take the required actions based on the reports.

128583.explanation: The two prostitution streets in The Hague, Geleen- and Doubletstraat, cause a lot of nuisance for residents. The many customers, onlookers, pimps, and drug dealers cause nuisance, insecurity, and crime. That must stop.

128584.explanation: Bonfires are part of The Hague. We want traditional Hague bonfires to remain possible under conditions of safety and public order.

128585.explanation: Our enforcement officers are on the front line when aggression, fights, or riots occur. To do their work well and safely, their equipment must be in order. In Scheveningen, this means pepper spray and a baton. Religious expressions do not belong with the uniform.

128586.explanation: In some areas, such as the Zeeheldenkwartier and Weimarstraat, there are too many coffeeshops too close together. This causes much nuisance for local residents. We want measures to counteract the overconcentration of coffeeshops.

128587.explanation: In recent years, there has been a stop on new hotels. Now that tourism and conferences have resumed after COVID, there is room again for new hotels. Not too many, with attention to quality and not in residential neighborhoods, but in tourist areas.

128588.explanation: The tax on owner-occupied houses should actually be reduced by 20%. The costs of homeownership have risen sharply in The Hague in recent years. We want to do something about that.

128589.explanation: Status holders have obtained the right to be in the Netherlands and deserve equal treatment. This means no priority over other housing seekers when allocating social rental homes. However, joint temporary housing options are possible to prevent them from becoming homeless.

128590.explanation: Vacancy should be avoided as much as possible, but is not always the fault of the homeowner. We want to prevent prolonged vacancy through flexible rules and quick permit procedures. The municipality should also hold owners accountable for vacant properties.

128591.explanation: There are big differences between homeless people. Some are Dutch, but many are (labor) migrants who have lost their jobs. The Hague VVD wants to focus on guidance toward care, work, or (for non-Dutch nationals) return to their homeland.

128592.explanation: There is a severe shortage of teachers, nurses, and police officers. This is partly because they find it difficult to obtain housing in The Hague. Therefore, we want to give them priority when allocating social rental housing.

128593.explanation: Many people are attached to their neighborhood. We want people to have the opportunity to stay in their neighborhood if they have to move. For example, if couples separate, but also young people leaving home or elderly people who want to downsize.

128594.explanation: Overcrowding is a major problem in parts of the city. Labor migrants rent a mattress in overcrowded apartments at high prices. This is unhealthy, unsafe, and causes serious nuisance. We want to give the Pandbrigade more and stricter enforcement powers.

128595.explanation: Victims of the childcare benefits scandal deserve support. They can continue to count on the municipality within the established agreements.

128596.explanation: Social assistance is for those who need a safety net. Fraud with social assistance or other arrangements is unfair and must not be allowed. By controlling this well, we prevent unnecessary societal costs.

128597.explanation: In some shopping streets, stores are barely accessible due to the large number of parked bicycles. By providing good bicycle parking spots, we keep sidewalks passable and stores accessible.

128598.explanation: In residential streets, a maximum speed of 30 km/h is appropriate. Important main roads remain at 50 km/h so that people can get in and out of the city properly. Good flow on these roads also prevents shortcut traffic through residential streets.

128599.explanation: In many neighborhoods, people have to search for a parking spot every evening. Where there are too few parking spaces, the municipality cannot simply remove spots. Parking spaces may only be removed if an underground parking garage is built. This then creates space for greenery.

128600.explanation: To make public transport more attractive, popular destinations must be well accessible by public transport. This requires investment in public transport. People will then be more likely to leave the car at home sometimes. For example, the Binckhorst, Kijkduin, and an extra stop in Leidschenveen.

128601.explanation: Nothing is free. The 'free' public transport for children costs the city millions annually. We want to encourage children to cycle instead. Cycling is healthy and does not burden the city with a large bill.

128602.explanation: On many straight, long roads through the city, people drive far too fast and accelerate recklessly. Noise cameras ensure that those who commit these offenses are caught. According to The Hague VVD, we should use them more often and in more places.

128603.explanation: Support among residents is a condition for The Hague VVD for introducing paid parking. Without support, no paid parking.

128604.explanation: With a personal budget (pgb), people can have control over their life and care. This also involves duties and responsibilities. The pgb should be available to those who can manage it, possibly with support from professionals.

128605.explanation: Currently, children and parents get stuck in long procedures and endless waiting lists. We want to ensure through agreements with referrers that every child quickly finds the right path through youth care.

128606.explanation: Smoking is unhealthy and it is important that every child learns this. We believe people should make their own choices. But we do not want children to grow up with the image that smoking is normal, and certainly not to be exposed to secondhand smoke during outdoor play. Therefore, smoking is not allowed at play areas.

128607.explanation: Quick debt assistance is very important. There are conditions that people must cooperate with.

128608.explanation: Healthcare costs are very high and continue to rise due to aging. To ensure that people who really need home care also receive it, we must also prevent abuse.

128609.explanation: With a limited personal contribution, there are fewer no-shows and transport is more often on time.

128610.explanation: At a minimum, Bronovo must remain an important care location, with general practitioners, other primary care, diagnostics, and specialized outpatient clinics.

128611.explanation: Many labor migrants from The Hague work (far) outside The Hague, such as in our neighboring municipality Westland. We want to intensify talks with the municipality of Westland to house labor migrants there, near their work.

128612.explanation: We want to invest in extra greenery and extra trees and allocate additional funds for this. We do not want this investment to depend on how many trees are cut down. However, for every tree cut down, a new tree must be planted.

128613.explanation: Such unrealistic demands hinder the construction of new homes. Therefore, for projects with more than 100 homes, we want 15% social housing, 25% mid-range rental, 25% affordable home ownership, and 35% free market. This way, construction will start quickly and more affordable owner-occupied homes will become available.

128614.explanation: Angling is a wonderful sport that many residents of The Hague enjoy with care and pleasure. From the canals to the Harbour Heads: as far as the Hague VVD is concerned, there is plenty of room for fishing.

128615.explanation: The Hague has no rural area, so this statement concerns other municipalities. They have their own municipal council that can decide on new construction. Construction in neighboring municipalities can help alleviate the housing shortage. We will have discussions with neighboring municipalities about this.

128616.explanation: Preventing youth from causing nuisance or getting involved in crime is important and should be funded. But currently, there is insufficient assessment of whether projects are effective. By cancelling projects that don't work, money can be saved.

128556.explanation: Municipal communication must be clearer, more accessible, and faster. Residents should not receive unclear letters from the municipality and should receive help if the letters are not clear.

128557.explanation: Policies must be developed together with residents. Through a randomly selected citizen council, the municipality can be assisted with decision-making on important decisions, creating more support.

128558.explanation: It is important that everyone can be physically active, as this promotes health. Therefore, we believe the municipality should focus on grassroots sports and sports in the neighborhood. Moreover, top-level sports are already funded nationally.

128559.explanation: Each neighborhood must have sufficient natural playgrounds without toxic materials. Where playgrounds do not yet exist, they should be installed. In neighborhoods where playgrounds already exist, they can be improved, for example by using less artificial grass and more natural materials.

128560.explanation: Everyone should be able to exercise sufficiently and participate socially. This can be achieved through small-scale neighborhood activities and by supporting sports clubs.

128561.explanation: Top sports events may sound appealing but cost millions of public money and cause nuisance. These prestige events have a major impact on nature, often cause traffic chaos, and result in noise pollution.

128562.explanation: City marketing and regional promotion cost a lot of money that could be better spent on sustainable objectives. The Hague will stop pure marketing and use the funds for tangible improvements for residents.

128563.explanation: Large conferences cost a lot of taxpayers' money and bring little benefit to the residents of The Hague. The conference sector is private and often involves greenhouse gas emissions due to flying visitors. Therefore, The Hague should not invest millions of public money in this.

128564.explanation: Thanks to the Party for the Animals, The Hague is the first municipality to ban fossil fuel advertising in public spaces. To tackle the climate crisis, we should not actively encourage the use of fossil fuels in public spaces.

128565.explanation: Sometimes the municipality takes a long time to issue simple permits, but sometimes entrepreneurs apply for permits that have significant impacts on livability, nature, and the environment. We believe these interests weigh heavily and must be carefully considered when granting or denying a permit. Therefore, entrepreneurs should not always receive permits quickly.

128566.explanation: Overfishing the sea is not sustainable, and fishing is a dead-end sector. The municipality should stop facilitating and promoting fishing. Fishing in Scheveningen must be phased out. The municipality should invest in the transition to sustainable business activities.

128567.explanation: The population is growing, and the municipality is getting more responsibilities. It is important that the number of civil servants grows with the tasks the municipality must perform. Civil servants are essential to ensure care, public spaces, and services are in order.

128568.explanation: The Hague must be a place where refugees have the right to dignified treatment. There is currently a created refugee crisis because the national government has reduced the number of reception places. It is important that every municipality contributes its fair share to finding a solution.

128569.explanation: Companies must comply with the law and take measures to save energy. Municipalities should tackle companies that do not comply. Polluting companies must be excluded and sustainable companies encouraged.

128570.explanation: Gambling is highly addictive and can cause people serious financial problems. Therefore, it must not be allowed for casinos to remain open unlimited hours.

128571.explanation: All (social) rental homes must be properly insulated and made sustainable. To force landlords and housing corporations to comply, it is necessary to be able to intervene and prohibit renting homes by 2030. Owners must invest in their properties.

128572.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate fully in society. Accessibility is a prerequisite. Therefore, all beach accesses must be accessible to everyone.

128573.explanation: Waste containers help reduce litter on the streets. This contributes to a clean and healthy living environment. It also better protects urban animals from suffocation or eating (for them) toxic food.

128574.explanation: It would be wonderful if previously filled-in canals were reopened. This makes the city more beautiful, is better for climate adaptation, and nicer for animals. Hague canals should once again be places where animals like to swim, bathe, and forage.

128575.explanation: The air quality in The Hague is poor and causes lung problems and heart attacks. Thanks to the Party for the Animals, the most polluting vans and trucks are now banned. This also reduces the amount of nitrogen entering protected nature.

128576.explanation: Seagulls belong in a city by the sea. They have the right to display their natural behavior. Therefore, people should not disturb them during breeding. Furthermore, the municipality cannot permit relocating nests since that is governed by the province.

128577.explanation: Libraries fulfill an important social function. Pupils and students must be able to use them, including evenings and weekends, to study.

128578.explanation: The Hague has grown into a city with many students. Unfortunately, the housing supply for students has not grown sufficiently. The municipality must provide more (shared) student housing and address sometimes poor living conditions.

128579.explanation: Every child should have a place at school. For various reasons, children may stay at home. The municipality must investigate the best way to help these children so they can still follow education that suits them.

128580.explanation: The budget for arts and culture must be structurally increased. Not only to expand cultural offerings but also to ensure fair pay in the cultural sector. The budget should reach at least a level comparable to other major cities.

128581.explanation: Libraries play a major role in promoting reading enjoyment and fulfill an important social function. We invest in sufficient libraries, including smaller neighborhood libraries, such as in Moerwijk and Mariahoeve, and branches of the Library at school.

128582.explanation: It is important that everyone feels safe on the street and is safe. Those causing insecurity must be addressed. More pruning or more lighting often does not increase safety. It disturbs animals' nocturnal rhythms and causes species like bats to disappear. The Party for the Animals wants to tackle the core problem so everyone can walk safely while protecting animals' habitats.

128583.explanation: Sex work has existed throughout time. Closing existing designated sex streets drives sex work underground and encourages human trafficking. We want to significantly improve the situation on Doublet and Geleen streets.

128584.explanation: The firewood stacks currently cost a lot of money and cause much air pollution. Therefore, we want an alternative to the firewood stacks in Scheveningen, Duindorp, and Laak due to harmful effects on the environment and people's health.

128585.explanation: Our community enforcement officers are committed to the city's livability. The police respond to violence and public order disturbances. Batons and pepper spray do not fit the role of enforcement officers, as they are not trained for this. The use of weapons can provoke violence instead.

128586.explanation: There are not too many coffeeshops in The Hague. However, there is an oversupply of coffeeshops in the Regentesse Quarter (Weimarstraat) and the Zeehelden Quarter. Therefore, the municipality must ensure these coffeeshops are relocated.

128587.explanation: In Scheveningen and downtown, the number of hotels is already large, and tourism causes nuisance and pressure on the living environment. Also, many hotels have low occupancy. No new hotels should be built. The remaining space should be used for housing.

128588.explanation: The Hague has a low property tax compared to other cities. To invest in greening and sustainability, it is desirable to slightly increase this tax for the most expensive houses.

128589.explanation: Without priority, status holders are forced to continue living in asylum seekers' centers (AZCs). This blocks the flow, causing new asylum seekers to sleep in inhumane conditions (such as tents). Status holders have a disadvantage in registration time and therefore receive priority.

128590.explanation: More housing starts with tackling vacancy. Thanks in part to the efforts of the Party for the Animals, The Hague will finally have a vacancy ordinance in 2026. This allows unnecessary vacancy to be fined and more properties, such as above shops, to be used for housing.

128591.explanation: A home is the foundation to develop further. People without a roof over their heads must be helped first. Instead of sleeping cold in a forest, we want to assist these people in obtaining housing.

128592.explanation: Everyone deserves an affordable home. Attention must be paid to the most vulnerable groups, such as the homeless. Differentiating based on someone's profession is undesirable and only worsens the housing crisis for others.

128593.explanation: Everyone deserves affordable housing. Attention must be paid to the most vulnerable groups, such as people without housing. Making distinctions based on where residents previously lived only worsens the housing crisis for others.

128594.explanation: The livability of a neighborhood is important. Illegal room occupancy does not contribute to livability and therefore the municipality should strictly control it. Besides causing nuisance, illegal room occupancy also leads to proven dangerous situations.

128595.explanation: The consequences of the childcare benefit scandal are still impacting some residents: much injustice has been done and the aftermath has gotten out of hand. These residents deserve quick and generous compensation, for which the municipality must advocate strongly.

128596.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits often do not choose this voluntarily. Checking them for fraud causes unnecessary stress and frustration. The municipality could better encourage them to find work or volunteer, for example at the food bank.

128597.explanation: In many neighborhoods, bicycles are now placed all over the sidewalks. This causes nuisance. More bicycle racks should be installed in these places. Sidewalks should be kept free for pedestrians and wheelchair users.

128598.explanation: Driving 30 km/h in residential areas significantly improves traffic safety. In addition, it creates more space for greening and contributes to cleaner air.

128599.explanation: We must focus on greening The Hague. The municipality should eliminate unnecessary parking spaces. These can be transformed into children's playgrounds, flower-rich verges, small parks, or bicycle parking spots. This way, we make room for clean air and a green, healthy environment.

128600.explanation: The Hague is growing and must invest more in public transport. We need to invest in better public transport to the Binckhorst and Southwest. This literally clears the way for high-quality, fast public transport. It is also good for air quality and the climate.

128601.explanation: Thanks to the Party for the Animals, public transport is now free for children. This must continue. This encourages parents to leave the car at home and choose cleaner transport. Additionally, it would be good if public transport were free for people with the lowest incomes.

128602.explanation: The municipality must tackle sources of noise nuisance in residential areas. Noise nuisance can cause stress and harm health by, for example, disrupting sleep. With noise cameras, we improve livability.

128603.explanation: It is important that paid parking is introduced citywide. This way, everyone contributes to the use of public space for cars. It also encourages more use of bicycles and public transport.

128604.explanation: People with disabilities in The Hague should have freedom of choice regarding the care they receive. Care must be tailored to individual needs. It should be easier for people with disabilities to receive a personal budget (pgb) to obtain that care.

128605.explanation: Investment must be made in youth care in The Hague. Reducing the long waiting lists in The Hague will allow more children to be helped. This gives them a better future.

128606.explanation: The Hague must aim for a smoke-free and vape-free generation. Therefore, smoking and vaping must no longer be allowed near schools, on children's playgrounds, at sports facilities, and in parks.

128607.explanation: People with debts must be helped. This can assist people in escaping poverty. It is important that this help is accessible and focused on prevention.

128608.explanation: Residents receiving home care do so for good reason. It is important that those who need it receive support, not extra scrutiny.

128609.explanation: Good student transport is important. Students who use it depend on it to get to their educational place. For this basic need, it is undesirable to ask for a personal contribution.

128610.explanation: Bronovo is a well-known hospital in The Hague. It is unfortunate that this hospital is being used less and less. With a growing population, it is desirable that Bronovo remains open. This way, everyone who needs care and help can get it without long waiting lists.

128611.explanation: Everyone in The Hague has the right to good, affordable housing. However, labor migrants are mainly placed in surrounding municipalities such as Westland. Companies should invest in housing there instead of shifting this problem onto The Hague.

128612.explanation: Stop cutting trees! It is important that The Hague becomes greener. Therefore, we tighten the tree-cutting criteria and ensure that if a tree is cut down, it is replaced by two new trees. Existing trees must also be given space to grow and age.

128613.explanation: More affordable housing is needed, especially in expensive neighborhoods. In those neighborhoods, where currently less than 30% of homes are social rental housing, at least 50% social rental housing must be included in new construction. This will reduce segregation in The Hague. Additionally, it is important that, alongside social rental housing, affordable housing is available for everyone in The Hague.

128614.explanation: Fishing causes enormous animal suffering. Many fish that are released after being caught still die. It is unacceptable that animals endure much pain for human pleasure. Therefore, fishing must be banned in The Hague.

128615.explanation: We focus on building homes within the existing urban area. We do not opt for building in the large green areas around the city but preserve these green lungs.

128616.explanation: Youth workers do important work as role models. They also help prevent problems among youth and are valuable to schools. This results in fewer security incidents. Therefore, we do not cut budgets for youth work.

128556.explanation: The municipality is there for every resident. Some information is complex and technical, but it is still the municipality’s task not to send citizens unintelligible letters.

128557.explanation: There are participation guidelines that we must adhere to; everyone involved should be able to participate. Ultimately, the municipal council is the representative body in the city that must make the interest assessment.

128558.explanation: Top-level sports and grassroots sports go hand in hand. The CDA wants to invest in both.

128559.explanation: In the past four years, partly thanks to the CDA, 400 extra playgrounds have been added. The CDA wants to focus on additional play spaces for children with disabilities in the future.

128560.explanation: The CDA has been committed for years to more sport and sports events in The Hague.

128561.explanation: The CDA has long supported top sports events. We continue to support sports clubs. As far as the CDA is concerned, the 'Beachstadion' event should become permanent.

128562.explanation: Tourism and conferences are important economic drivers of our city. We must invest in these.

128563.explanation: The Hague is an international city; conferences are an economic driver. The CDA wants to focus on improving the quality of these conferences and meetings.

128564.explanation: The CDA was not in favor of this ban and still is not.

128565.explanation: The CDA supports eliminating unnecessary delaying rules for permits. Simple applications like dormer windows should be processed faster.

128566.explanation: Fishing belongs to Scheveningen and is a sustainable protein source. This should be promoted with more pride.

128567.explanation: Civil servants are essential for good service. Improving service while cutting civil servant staff is unrealistic. The CDA does want a shift from policy civil servants to service and execution roles.

128568.explanation: The Hague must meet its legal obligations. However, the allocation law does not consider that The Hague is the most densely populated city in the Netherlands. This law demands more from The Hague than from other cities. The CDA wants this to be taken into account in the law.

128569.explanation: The CDA supports encouraging entrepreneurs to use energy more sustainably. Coercion often leads to resistance, which is not conducive to the ultimate goal of a more sustainable city.

128570.explanation: Gambling addiction is a major problem; it is illogical to push people with gambling problems further into ruin.

128571.explanation: There is a large housing shortage in The Hague. We encourage better insulation of rental homes, but banning rentals also affects tenants in our city and worsens the housing shortage.

128572.explanation: Our beach must be accessible to everyone.

128573.explanation: Where space is available, underground containers should be installed.

128574.explanation: The CDA has been a strong supporter of reopening our canals for years.

128576.explanation: Seagulls cause nuisance and pose a public health risk. Relocating nests can help reduce waste nuisance in The Hague.

128578.explanation: In collaboration with student housing organizations, we are looking at solving the shortage of rooms. The CDA wants to allow subdividing houses for student accommodation.

128579.explanation: Education is the foundation for every child in society. Children belong in school.

128580.explanation: The CDA supports many beautiful cultural projects in the city. At this moment, sufficient funds go to art and culture. In other areas of the municipality, the funding is more urgently needed than for this topic.

128582.explanation: The CDA is committed to the safety of women. Besides street lighting and pruning, the CDA also wants to invest in early detection of violence against women within the home.

128583.explanation: Unregulated prostitution often leads to human trafficking and exploitation. The CDA wants more attention for supporting women who want to exit prostitution.

128584.explanation: The CDA is dedicated to preserving the beautiful traditions of our city. Bonfires, provided they are safe, are a nice part of our municipal culture.

128585.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers (BOA’s) should have the ability to defend themselves. The monopoly on violence remains with the police; the baton is only for self-defense.

128586.explanation: The CDA opposes coffeeshops in residential neighborhoods and near schools, and demands strict enforcement. The CDA works to reduce the number of existing coffeeshops.

128587.explanation: If the occupancy rate of hotels in The Hague exceeds 80%, there is room for new hotels in the city.

128588.explanation: Municipal taxes are already high enough. The CDA opposes increases in the property tax (ozb), except for inflation adjustments.

128589.explanation: The CDA believes we must meet our legal obligations, but at the same time the pressure on our city is disproportionately high. The Hague is densely populated and has no room for expansion. The distribution method must be reconsidered.

128590.explanation: Such a generic measure is too simplistic. Owners often do not choose to leave their homes vacant for a long time but face regulations that delay development. This can only apply if no development is planned and intentional vacancy exists.

128598.explanation: In residential streets, we advocate a safe speed of 30 km/h with priority for staying, walking, and cycling. For through and district-connecting roads, this remains 50 km/h.

128599.explanation: Parking facilities are scarce. Where parking pressure is high, parking spaces should not disappear, as there is obviously a need. In neighborhoods where this is not the case, it can be considered.

128600.explanation: The CDA supports new tram lines, for example, a tram line in Southwest, perpendicular to the coast.

128609.explanation: This must be done based on capacity to bear.

128610.explanation: By stating this, we raise the expectation that this is also an option, but it is not. In redevelopment, we as a municipality must insist that care facilities return so that the neighborhood’s level of amenities remains adequate.

128611.explanation: Seasonal workers employed in horticulture in neighboring municipalities should be housed there.

128613.explanation: Such a requirement would delay new construction and in some cases make projects unfeasible. The CDA focuses on more mobility in the housing market and no one-sided focus on the social segment. By realizing homes for mobility, every home seeker benefits.

128614.explanation: The CDA always supports sports and clubs. We therefore will not advocate a ban.

128556.explanation: We believe multilingual support is necessary to help people become independent and find their way. Not everyone has the aptitude to learn a new language.

128557.explanation: DENK supports promoting participation and believes that important decisions should be made together with residents. As stated in our election program: “Residents get real influence on decisions.”

128558.explanation: Elite sports are important for our city and our Haagse talents. Investment is already made, and in our view, no additional funding is needed.
