If more money goes to sports, it should go to grassroots sports, more artificial turf fields, and making sports more affordable for everyone.

128559.explanation: In our opinion, better playgrounds should be provided where needed, for example where playgrounds do not meet needs or are outdated. The quality of playgrounds also deserves significant improvement in some neighborhoods.

128560.explanation: Sports are for everyone and should be facilitated as such. DENK believes we should do more for sports other than the “standard” ones that get less attention now. A sports activity where people encounter different sports is a good idea!

128561.explanation: A top-level sports event adds value to The Hague as a city. However, we believe we should not allocate many additional funds for this, funding should come from existing elite sports resources.

128562.explanation: We believe enough people now find their way to The Hague, which is also good for the economy. Attention should go to attracting international organizations and conferences.

128563.explanation: Conferences and international meetings contribute to The Hague’s economy. For international meetings, we must ensure neighborhoods suffer as little disturbance as possible. Months-long closures have a big impact. We must clearly assess what the city can handle.

128564.explanation: We see advantages in banning certain types of advertising; however, we must not go too far. Advertising for beach holidays should be allowed in our opinion.

128565.explanation: Entrepreneurs benefit from fast service and processing of permit applications. DENK has already strongly advocated this in recent years; we want to continue this approach!

128566.explanation: In recent years, DENK has shown that economic policy is stronger when the municipality really cooperates with entrepreneurs. These results show that The Hague progresses when we choose local investment.

128567.explanation: We should aim for efficiency, not necessarily fewer civil servants.

128568.explanation: DENK stands for humane reception of people fleeing unsafe situations in their home countries. We take our responsibility and contribute our part, as other municipalities should do as well.

128569.explanation: When saving and making sustainable choices, the entrepreneur should remain in control. The municipality should primarily stimulate, for example with attractive subsidies.

128570.explanation: Gambling leads to many problems. We should not facilitate it by making casinos or gambling halls more attractive to visit.

128571.explanation: In our view, sanctions should follow if landlords do not address poorly insulated homes, for example fines. Regarding rental bans, we look at national policy, where rental bans do not yet seem to be relevant.

128572.explanation: “All” is very specific; what is important for DENK is that there are sufficient accessible entrances available.

128573.explanation: The Hague has a waste problem. DENK wants underground waste transport to collection stations to reduce nuisance and litter. In addition, we will strengthen waste services and enforcement, especially in vulnerable neighborhoods.

128574.explanation: That seems like a nice goal but also costs quite a lot. The investments for this could be substantial, money that we believe is better spent on improving affordability and quality of life.

128575.explanation: We see climate change as one of the biggest challenges of our time and as a responsibility towards future generations.

128576.explanation: If this is allowed, more will follow. Humans and nature must be in balance, and the ecosystem must not be disturbed. If there is nuisance from seagulls, we need to look at the causes, such as additional waste deposits, and address those.

128577.explanation: Municipal buildings should be used to the fullest for residents. This contributes to promoting education and reduces pressure on schools and homes. Therefore, we also want to offer study and learning spaces outside regular days and times.

128578.explanation: We want this precisely because a new university location is opening. Considering the further expansion of other educational institutions, that growth must be balanced with sufficient facilities and suitable housing so the city remains livable for both students and residents.

128579.explanation: There is a care letter about children who stay at home. As a municipality, we must do more to better align the current system with what children and parents actually need. This requires better guidance, more support, and actively facilitating and offering suitable solutions.

128580.explanation: We want art and culture to become much more accessible. This requires an appropriate budget to actually enable this. Our election program therefore states: culture connects people and offers space for creativity and development.

128581.explanation: There are neighborhoods where the demand for this is great. We also see that libraries are important meeting places: people come together there, attend voluntary language classes, and gain knowledge. Precisely for this reason, it is necessary to further expand this as a stimulus and increase visibility.

128582.explanation: Safety goes beyond combating crime. It is about neighborhoods that are clean, safe, and inclusive. Neighborhoods where residents feel seen, where women can walk the streets without fear.

128583.explanation: Window prostitution does not fit a safe and livable city. It is outdated that streets are used for this purpose when that space can be used differently. We see that living and conducting business in the immediate vicinity becomes less attractive.

128584.explanation: Traditional bonfires, such as those on Scheveningen beach, are valuable moments of togetherness. They are days when residents and visitors come together, meet each other, and the diversity of the city becomes visible. That is why we see this as a moment to cherish.

128585.explanation: Enforcement by municipal officials (boa’s) must focus on de-escalation, addressing issues, and prevention. In situations involving (threats of) violence or serious public order disruption, the police take charge, with the appropriate powers, training, and responsibility structure.

128586.explanation: The number of coffeeshops should be reduced, especially in vulnerable neighborhoods where safety is under pressure. Residents report dark spots, nuisance, and drug waste. Fewer coffeeshops can contribute to more peace and less attraction.

128587.explanation: More hotels strengthen The Hague as an international city (tourism, conferences, diplomacy) and create jobs. Hotels also boost local shops, markets, and hospitality. This strengthens the neighborhood economy.

128588.explanation: Due to rising costs, more and more people are financially exhausted. Even full-time workers see their purchasing power decline and find it increasingly difficult to cover basic expenses.

128589.explanation: There is a housing shortage. Blaming newcomers as the cause of the housing shortage is both incorrect and harmful to social cohesion. The solution lies in increasing the availability of affordable housing for everyone.

128590.explanation: The Vacancy Act applies here, and we see that strict enforcement is needed to combat vacancy and deterioration. A long-term vacant property damages the neighborhood: it attracts nuisance, lowers the feeling of safety, and harms livability.

128591.explanation: Priority helps to sustainably break homelessness and prevents emergency shelters from becoming a permanent solution.

128592.explanation: The municipality can give priority for social housing to teachers, nurses, and police officers because there are significant shortages in these sectors. If living in the city remains feasible, it becomes more attractive to work and stay here.

128593.explanation: Neighborhood residents must have priority for homes in their own area so they are not pushed out. This keeps families near their network, school, and work, and gives their children a fair chance to continue living nearby.

128594.explanation: Pressure is increasing and tenants are being exploited with excessively high rents and too small/unsafe rooms. Illegal room occupancy also increases nuisance and illegal activities.

128595.explanation: Victims have the right to recovery, guidance, and new opportunities. It is not only about financial compensation but about progress through better housing, educational opportunities, and chances for employment. DENK wants the municipality to assist these families humanely and without barriers.

128596.explanation: Addressing fraud is very important, including fraud with social assistance benefits. We must be careful not to suspect people with social assistance benefits of fraud more often and not cause undue hardship through increased controls.

128597.explanation: Where necessary. This should not come at the expense of car parking spaces.

128598.explanation: Many roads can handle 30 km/h, which also improves traffic safety. As far as we're concerned, this should apply to residential streets where traffic safety improves and flow is not impaired. So not immediately in all residential areas.

128599.explanation: We already have a shortage of parking spaces. Investing in green spaces and parks is good, but not at the cost of parking spaces.

128600.explanation: Good connections are needed in all neighborhoods. Investing in public transport is necessary for accessibility.

128601.explanation: Free public transport was one of DENK's achievements in this coalition period. A very successful pilot that deserves continuation, preferably even for older age groups.

128602.explanation: Noise nuisance from traffic harms livability and health. With more controls, including noise cameras, we tackle notorious noise makers and encourage quieter driving behavior.

128603.explanation: A caveat is that paid parking has already been introduced in almost all of The Hague. Several parties ensured this during the past coalition period. As far as we are concerned, the focus should be mainly on the time windows.

128604.explanation: When someone is entitled, we must reduce barriers to application and allocation as much as possible.

128605.explanation: Waiting lists are growing and that is unacceptable: children and families must receive appropriate help on time. Therefore, a concrete goal for 2030 is needed, so the municipality can steer more actively on increased capacity and faster referrals.

128606.explanation: Smoking and vaping around schools and playgrounds must be prohibited so that children see it less and are less likely to normalize it.

128607.explanation: People with debts must gain faster and easier access to debt counseling so that problems do not escalate further.

128608.explanation: If someone is entitled to home care, that person is demonstrably in need of help. Then the municipality should not operate from distrust and 'hunt' for reasons to withdraw assistance.

128609.explanation: Care taxis and student transport are specifically for children and families who need them. It often concerns parents of children with care needs who already face extra costs for care, guidance, and adaptations. A personal contribution makes support less accessible.

128610.explanation: The municipality must insist that Bronovo becomes a fully-fledged hospital again, with emergency care and outpatient clinics. For vulnerable groups, other hospitals are often too far away or hard to reach.

128611.explanation: The housing must be appropriate and dignified.

128612.explanation: Preferably one sustainable, maturing tree that fits the local ecosystem, rather than two plantings with little chance of survival or ecological value.

128613.explanation: Denk advocates for a distribution of: 40% social housing, 40% affordable middle-rent/owner-occupied homes, and 20% higher-end segment. In neighborhoods where we currently have very few, this can be higher.

128614.explanation: Angling is a permitted recreational activity that offers many residents relaxation and nature experiences. A total ban is disproportionate. If there are issues with animal welfare, litter, or nuisance, the municipality should regulate and enforce rules in a targeted manner.

128615.explanation: We need housing, also outside the city boundaries, provided it is affordable and has good public transport connections and facilities. Otherwise students, starters, families, and seniors get stuck, and homelessness increases.

128616.explanation: Youth work prevents more serious problems and ultimately also reduces higher costs. Therefore, the municipality should not cut but rather invest in youth work, especially in vulnerable neighborhoods.

128556.explanation: The municipality must make letters and emails understandable for everyone. This ensures equal access to information and services regardless of background, language level, or disability. Clear language helps reduce inequality and exclusion.

128557.explanation: A citizens' assembly can be valuable but only if everyone can genuinely participate. Without proper conditions, mainly highly educated people take part and marginalized groups remain invisible. Conditions include childcare, compensation, and support.

128558.explanation: The municipality must first ensure that sports are accessible, safe, and inclusive for everyone. Amateur sports, facilities in neighborhoods, and support for vulnerable groups take priority.

128559.explanation: Playgrounds must be safe, green, fun, and challenging. There must be sufficient shelter so children and their parents or caregivers can spend longer periods there. Seating, waste bins, and public toilets are also important.

128560.explanation: Sports must be accessible to everyone regardless of origin, age, disability, or socioeconomic status. We stand for a city where everyone has the opportunity to participate in sports.

128561.explanation: The municipality must first ensure that sports are accessible, safe, and inclusive for everyone. Amateur sports, facilities in neighborhoods, and support for vulnerable groups take priority.

128562.explanation: We strive for a climate-neutral and just city. This means stopping the encouragement and attraction of fossil fuel companies or companies that merely shuffle money. We also cease promoting the city outside Europe because flying is harmful.

128563.explanation: The municipality should not grant subsidies to international conferences without sustainable impact or good labor conditions. Support only initiatives that create jobs, pay fairly, and are sustainable. Domestic events deserve priority.

128564.explanation: Advertising for flight holidays and diesel cars encourages people to choose more polluting options. The ban helps The Hague become climate-neutral and promotes sustainable choices for everyone.

128565.explanation: Less bureaucracy is important, but permits must be granted carefully. The municipality must assess applications based on fair wages, sustainability, and social entrepreneurship.

128566.explanation: Scheveningen and the harbor are important to The Hague and have a rich tradition. We acknowledge this. But the municipality does not need to actively promote fish. We also do not promote other specific dietary choices.

128567.explanation: The municipality must serve the residents. Investing in sufficient, well-trained staff is necessary to adequately assist residents and maintain service levels.

128568.explanation: We continue accommodating Ukrainians, status holders, and asylum seekers, as at the Sportlaan location.

128569.explanation: Businesses must contribute to a climate-neutral city. Strict control applies especially to major polluters, while small entrepreneurs should be supported in energy savings through subsidies and advice to keep sustainable business feasible.

128571.explanation: Tenants have the right to a healthy and affordable home. Poorly insulated homes cause high costs and unhealthy conditions. Landlords who ignore this must be required to insulate or be prohibited from renting out.

128572.explanation: A city of equal opportunities is also a physically accessible city. People with disabilities too often encounter practical barriers. This leads to exclusion, which is unacceptable.

128573.explanation: Everyone must be able to dispose of their waste close to home. This can be achieved by adding more underground containers (ORACs) and more waste bins in neighborhoods.

128574.explanation: Restoring filled-in canals strengthens the character of the inner city and helps absorb rainwater. Open canals make the city greener, cooler, and more livable.

128575.explanation: We ensure small entrepreneurs are helped with subsidies to comply with environmental zone regulations. We mainly focus on major consumers who currently escape regulation and end exemptions for businesses in the Scheveningen harbor.

128576.explanation: A livable city is also one that protects animals. From pets to wild animals, from insects to birds: The Hague must be an animal-friendly city where care duty and respect for animals are central. We want nature-inclusive building with provisions for bats, swallows, and sparrows.

128577.explanation: Accessible study places in the evenings and weekends give young people space to develop. A quiet place to learn must be accessible for everyone.

128578.explanation: The Hague is a growing student city. We want students to afford housing so they can study, learn, and contribute to a vibrant city. New student houses help achieve this.

128579.explanation: The municipality must ensure that every child can attend school. Home-sitters receive personal guidance and extra support so no one falls through the cracks.

128580.explanation: The cultural budget is structurally increased by 22 million euros, so The Hague invests as much as other big cities. This ensures sufficient funding to support young creators, initiatives by Black people and people of color, and a diverse cultural offer alongside established institutions.

128581.explanation: We want a full-fledged library in every neighborhood. This gives everyone access to books, information, and knowledge.

128582.explanation: This can help women feel safer but does not solve the problem. Patriarchal violence requires prevention and education. From primary education onwards, structural work must be done to raise awareness about harmful stereotypes and gender roles.

128583.explanation: Sex work is work and should be treated as such by the government. We encourage self-organization initiatives and provide good support for sex workers who want to exit if desired.

128584.explanation: Bonfires of joy are an old tradition we should cherish. They bring people together but must be organized well and safely. This should be allowed once a year.

128585.explanation: The municipality's focus must be on prevention, trust, and equality, not increasing repression. Therefore, we oppose expanding enforcement capacity. Prevention, education, and offering youth perspective-not stricter enforcement.

128586.explanation: The number of coffeeshops is currently unevenly distributed in the city, causing much traffic and sometimes nuisance. We want to take steps to better distribute coffeeshops across the city. We also support legalizing the cultivation and trade of soft drugs.

128587.explanation: Hotel freeze citywide: no new hotels or short-stay accommodations will be added in the coming four years.

128588.explanation: Property tax is an important source of revenue for the municipality. Those who own buildings can contribute, enabling the municipality to invest in good facilities for everyone.

128589.explanation: Status holders must receive priority for social rental housing. This prevents people from being sent from hotel to hotel. The current situation is expensive and unfair, mainly benefiting entrepreneurs, not the residents themselves.

128590.explanation: In The Hague alone, 20,000 homes stand empty. Many commercial spaces, shops, and office buildings have also been vacant for years; together, these buildings could shelter thousands of people again. Vacancy during housing shortages is inexplicable and unacceptable.

128591.explanation: Urgency for those who need it: homeless people and those in vulnerable housing situations (such as couch-surfing or unsafe networks) receive priority in housing. We formalize this in performance agreements with housing corporations.

128592.explanation: Many teachers, nurses, and police officers earn too much to qualify for social housing. Social rental housing must be fairly allocated to those who truly need it.

128593.explanation: The Hague is the most segregated city in the Netherlands. We want every neighborhood to be diverse in income, background, gender, and age.

128594.explanation: Illegal room occupancy is often a consequence of rent that is too expensive or of poor quality. We protect tenants from exploitation and ensure that housing is affordable and safe. Enforcement should target unscrupulous landlords, not vulnerable tenants.

128595.explanation: The municipality assists residents of The Hague who are victims of the benefits scandal through broad support. Many parents are unaware of the available support options. A clear overview will be provided with examples of what victims can access via broad support.

128596.explanation: Humanity must be paramount in supporting people with low income, benefits, or debts. Fines and possible recoveries for small errors in applications contribute to insecurity of existence.

128597.explanation: We encourage cycling by resolving bottlenecks, designing streets safely, and ensuring sufficient bike parking options near homes and destinations. We work together with residents and cyclists to make the solutions safe and attractive.

128598.explanation: The city has been designed for cars instead of people for years. Traffic safety and livability come first. Therefore, we make 30 km/h the standard speed limit throughout the city. It has been proven that accidents at 30 km/h are five times less likely to be fatal than at 50 km/h.

128599.explanation: We want the city to be livable and safe for people, not just for cars. Existing parking spaces remain, but no new ones will be added. This way, we maintain accessibility where needed and improve the living environment.

128600.explanation: Good, fast, and free public transport is essential for the city. This allows everyone to get everywhere. This means that in the coming years we must invest in new lines, speed up routes, or operate linked services during rush hour.

128601.explanation: Free public transport for children up to 12 years old must remain. Public transport is a public service, not a profit model. We want everyone to be able to travel for free. Good, fast, and free public transport is essential for the city.

128602.explanation: Noise nuisance mainly occurs at specific locations. Residents affected should be heard and targeted solutions can be implemented.

128603.explanation: Parking spaces occupy much public space and are often subsidized. With paid parking: the polluter pays. It helps to keep the city livable and accessible.

128604.explanation: The personal budget (pgb) is a legal right. Currently, the municipality places extra obstacles so that people more often have to use contracted institutions. As a result, the pgb is primarily accessible to highly educated people. We want good client support so that everyone who wants to can use a pgb.

128605.explanation: Children should not have to wait for the help they need now. Long waiting lists harm their health and future. The municipality must ensure timely youth care, with clear decisions, sufficient staff, and emergency help in acute problems. Money and cost savings should never be leading.

128606.explanation: Schools and playground organizations can make their own agreements about smoking and vaping. The municipality does not need to centrally prohibit this.

128607.explanation: Debts should not make life impossible for anyone. By making debt assistance faster and easier to access, we prevent debts from increasing and people from losing their homes or security of existence. We choose a people-centered approach where people are truly helped.

128608.explanation: It is already checked whether people receiving home care really need cleaning assistance. Stricter controls are unnecessary.

128609.explanation: Those who use a care taxi or student transport should not pay a fixed personal contribution. We believe contributions should be income-dependent.

128610.explanation: We understand the sentiment in this part of Benoordenhout, but financially and practically it is not feasible to make Bronovo a full-fledged hospital again. The shortage of (surgical) nurses is a major obstacle. Fortunately, patients can quickly be seen at the Westeinde Hospital.

128611.explanation: We do not want vulnerable labor migrants to depend on employers for their income and housing. Municipalities hosting companies that employ many labor migrants must independently ensure good housing. The Hague, as a regional municipality, has a role in this too.

128612.explanation: Trees should only be cut down if demonstrably unavoidable. The replanting obligation must be strictly enforced and monitored based on numbers and canopy volume. The larger the tree cut down, the more trees must be replanted.

128613.explanation: For new construction and transformation, at least 50% social housing is mandatory for projects of 20 homes or more. Within this, a part of the homes is reserved for homeless and roofless people.

128614.explanation: The Hague City Party wants a green and fair city. We target the real polluters, not individual residents. A ban on angling yields little and distracts from measures that do help nature and livability.

128615.explanation: There is a housing crisis in The Hague. We must enable new construction, also outside existing city boundaries, but always in a way that affects nature and green as little as possible.

128616.explanation: Youth work is a crucial preventive measure. Whether it concerns youth in Duindorp, Schilderswijk, or Houtwijk: a network of youth workers with short lines to other caregivers is important. This way, problems can be addressed early and youth can stay out of the justice system.

128556.explanation: It is self-evident that the municipality must communicate clearly. If someone receives a letter from the municipality and does not understand it, that person has the right to return it and receive a clearer letter.

128557.explanation: The SP is not in favor of citizen assemblies, which do not offer real influence to residents. We strengthen existing participation methods and support binding corrective referendums.

128558.explanation: The SP prefers to invest this money in broad sports facilities for the entire city. Top athletes also start as amateurs!

128559.explanation: Every child deserves a well-maintained and beautiful playground nearby. In some neighborhoods, there are insufficient play areas or existing playgrounds are poorly maintained.

128560.explanation: We expand the target group that can use the Ooievaarspas for a reduced rate or free sports participation to 150% of the minimum income. We invest in sports for women and people with disabilities, so everyone can play sports.

128561.explanation: The SP prefers to invest this money in broad sports facilities for our own residents. Top athletes also start as amateurs!

128562.explanation: The Hague benefits from supporting our local economy, not from attracting as many tourists and large fairs/events as possible. The SP therefore prefers to invest this money in our working-class neighborhoods and SMEs.

128563.explanation: The city gains little from large international conferences. The NATO summit, for example, caused much inconvenience for local businesses and the city. No new conference venues will be developed, and we will critically review the ownership of the World Forum.

128564.explanation: Advertising pollutes our neighborhoods and makes the city uglier. The SP believes that outdoor advertising should ultimately disappear entirely.

128565.explanation: The municipality should not stand in the way of local businesses. There will be one point of contact within the municipality for permits and subsidies.

128566.explanation: There must be space for fishing in the Scheveningen harbor, but the municipality must look at this sector realistically. It is not a given that fishing will have a major revival through large-scale municipal investments or promotion.

128567.explanation: The municipality should become less dependent on external hires, such as expensive consultants, by developing and retaining knowledge in-house. There are also shortages of municipal enforcement officers (boa's) and cleaners for public spaces, which the SP would like to address.

128568.explanation: We adhere to the Distribution Act and do not avoid our obligations. Every municipality must fairly accommodate its share. We also spread asylum seekers fairly throughout the city based on capacity.

128569.explanation: The municipality must make large-scale investments itself in building public heating and energy companies, and must support companies and households in sustainability efforts.

128570.explanation: We do not want The Hague to become a Las Vegas by the North Sea and therefore set much stricter requirements on gambling. Casinos and gambling halls cause long-term addiction and debt problems. We must not encourage gambling addiction by keeping casinos open permanently.

128571.explanation: We introduce rental permits throughout the city to ensure private tenants have access to good quality housing. We freeze rents on homes with energy labels E, F, and G. Landlords must ensure their homes are well insulated by 2030.

128572.explanation: The city and the beach belong to everyone and must therefore be accessible to all. We open a reporting point for inaccessible places in the city, so people can report if they encounter inaccessible public space design.

128573.explanation: All remaining above-ground containers will be replaced by underground ones as quickly as possible. In densely populated neighborhoods where waste is still placed on the street, a solution will be worked on as soon as possible. The municipality is experimenting with bins for small bulky waste next to ORACs.

128574.explanation: Canals help against heat stress and water nuisance. Moreover, they make the city more beautiful.

128575.explanation: This would not be fair to the many businesses that have already taken steps to make their vehicle fleets more sustainable. However, the municipality must pay a lot of attention to small business owners and self-employed professionals who struggle financially with this sustainability transition, and offer financial aid and exemptions.

128576.explanation: Gulls are an endangered species and we must act within the law. The best way to reduce nuisance caused by gulls is to place underground waste containers, keep public spaces clean, and prohibit feeding animals.

128577.explanation: It is important that we as a municipality ensure that everyone, regardless of their home situation, has a suitable study space. These spaces must be accessible to every student, regardless of their course.

128578.explanation: We make agreements with DUWO to build more student housing and to convert vacant buildings. The housing supply must also become more diverse, for example with shared facilities, instead of just studios.

128579.explanation: More and more children are staying home instead of going to school. The municipality must ensure appropriate educational facilities for everyone, including children of labor migrants, people with residence permits, and asylum seekers.

128580.explanation: Currently, subsidies for cultural institutions in The Hague are insufficient to approve all positively assessed applications. This mainly hurts small or new institutions. The SP advocates supporting vulnerable cultural initiatives and stricter evaluation for large institutions.

128581.explanation: Everyone deserves access to a local library. Some neighborhoods, such as Mariahoeve, still do not have a library. Those must be established as soon as possible.

128582.explanation: It is important that everyone feels safe on the streets. We will continue with the 'Safe Over Street' reporting point and adapt public spaces to improve unsafe spots as quickly as possible.

128583.explanation: Whether sex work locations should continue is up to the sex workers themselves, just like all entrepreneurs. They must be facilitated where necessary. At the same time, it is important that the municipality ensures the safety of the work and the environment, preferably in cooperation with sex workers.

128584.explanation: Now that the national fireworks ban takes effect from 2026, it is important that we support local New Year's celebrations. Therefore, we cooperate with the bonfires in Laak, Duindorp, and Scheveningen.

128585.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) must not be given extra weapons and police tasks belong to the police. National budget cuts for the police are no reason to give enforcement officers more powers. We focus on further integration and cooperation between enforcement and police.

128586.explanation: The current number of permits for coffeeshops will be maintained: we do not follow a policy of phasing out. However, we will counter overconcentration of coffeeshops in certain streets by proactively looking for suitable locations. The neighborhood will always be well informed.

128587.explanation: The Hague has a housing shortage and more than enough hotels. Many hotels stand empty for large parts of the year. It is irresponsible to use land that can be used for housing for more hotels. A stop on new hotels is also good for the existing hotels.

128588.explanation: To be able to invest in our neighborhoods, we must look at where cuts can be made, but also at which charges can possibly be increased. We strictly limit property tax increases to the average of the four major cities.

128589.explanation: It is not the fault of people with residence permits that others cannot find social rental housing. It is important to relieve the asylum chain and ensure people quickly get future prospects and can integrate into the neighborhood. The SP focuses on social housing so there is room for everyone.

128590.explanation: We implement a strict vacancy ordinance that makes vacant buildings come to the municipality's attention earlier. Owners are encouraged to rent out or sell these buildings. In extreme cases, such as decay, the municipality takes over to rent out themselves.

128591.explanation: The number of homeless people in The Hague is increasing. The simplest solution to this is housing. We therefore give homeless people priority in social housing. We use the ETHOS Light definition of lack of adequate, decent housing.

128592.explanation: Giving priority to certain professions does not offer a structural solution to the housing shortage or make these professions more attractive. Moreover, there are other professions we urgently need in the city, such as bus drivers, cleaners, and construction workers.

128593.explanation: Everyone should have the right to continue living in their familiar neighborhood, even if they have children or grow older. People who have lived in a neighborhood for at least five years should get priority for new housing in that neighborhood. This way, we nurture close-knit communities.

128594.explanation: We tackle landlords who exploit tenants firmly by introducing rental permits throughout the city and using the property inspection team to monitor this. We open a reporting point where people can report illegal rental practices to the municipality.

128595.explanation: The municipality has the knowledge and expertise to help victims. Especially when there is one place where victims can report for help, support can be provided effectively. The victims must be listened to carefully.

128596.explanation: People on welfare must be treated with trust. The municipality must provide guidance and facilitate work. We abolish the whistleblower hotline for welfare benefits. It is unnecessary and does not strengthen enforcement.

128597.explanation: The entire city must be accessible by bicycle. To prevent nuisance from bicycles in shopping streets, at stops, and near schools, enough bicycle parking spaces must be installed.

128598.explanation: The Hague must become more bicycle-friendly. Busy roads without segregated bicycle paths will be made a 30 km/h zone as soon as possible. This greatly reduces the chance of (fatal) accidents.

128599.explanation: Significant investment must be made in more greenery in the city, especially in paved neighborhoods such as Schilderswijk, Transvaal, and Laak. This does not necessarily have to replace parking spaces. Greenery lowers temperatures in summer and beautifies our neighborhoods.

128600.explanation: The municipality must invest heavily in public transport in The Hague, especially in working-class neighborhoods. Not only the city center must be well accessible. We continue with the Vliet Line, King's Corridor, and Southwest Land Corridor projects.

128601.explanation: Children up to 12 years old must be able to travel free on public transport. We extend this to youths up to 21, people 65 and older, homeless people, and everyone with an Ooievaarspas. Eventually, public transport will be free for everyone.

128602.explanation: Everyone has the right to a quiet and livable neighborhood. Noise nuisance must be minimized as much as possible.

128603.explanation: Paid parking should only be introduced based on support among residents and depending on parking pressure. This way, we reserve parking spaces for residents in neighborhoods with high parking pressure and avoid unnecessary costs in neighborhoods where parking pressure is low.

128604.explanation: Residents must receive quick decisions on their requests for help. They should not wait weeks in uncertainty about whether their application will be approved. Applications must be processed faster without waiting lists.

128605.explanation: It is important that youth care is accessible to everyone. Therefore, it must be available in places where young people are. There are fewer and fewer social workers at schools. This must be reversed.

128606.explanation: Every child has the right to a smoke-free school and play environment.

128607.explanation: The municipality works to shorten debt counseling processes (and the lead-up to them). We stop commercial debt administration and the municipality takes debt administration into its own hands.

128608.explanation: People with care needs must be treated with trust. Those who need care should receive it without hassle and distrust from the government.

128609.explanation: Student transportation must be organized by the municipality, with attention to both drivers and passengers. Student transportation is crucial for the care and education of pupils, so it is unfair if people have to pay for it themselves.

128610.explanation: The Bronovo Hospital must remain open as a weekly hospital after 2030 as well.

128611.explanation: A regular rental contract, including the rights and security that come with it, must be the norm, also for labor migrants. Rental agreements should be made with a regular landlord such as a housing corporation, not a temp agency.

128612.explanation: For every tree that is cut down, two trees will be planted within six months. This ensures that the total 'canopy surface' (the leaf surface) of city trees will ultimately increase.

128613.explanation: For years, too few social rental homes have been added. It is not only time to correct that, but to make up for lost ground. Therefore, at least 50% of the new-build homes must be social housing. And social must really mean social: no disguised mid-rent or so-called affordable owner-occupied housing.

128614.explanation: Fishing is a relaxing outdoor activity for many residents, so the SP thinks it goes too far to introduce a total ban at once. However, it is good to keep having conversations with residents about the harmful effects on animals.

128615.explanation: There are many beautiful and important nature areas around the city. We must not build them over, so we can preserve them for all current and future residents of The Hague.

128616.explanation: The SP wants more professional youth workers in community centers. Young people can turn to them with problems they cannot discuss at home. Community centers, youth workers, and schools must work closely together on this.

128556.explanation: Of course, in understandable language but only in Dutch.

128557.explanation: The PVV prefers neighborhood or city referenda so everyone's opinion counts. Citizen assemblies per neighborhood are a good participation option though.

128558.explanation: No tax money for Top Sports in principle, but there should be an ADO fan shop in The Hague.

128559.explanation: The necessity should be determined per neighborhood. Livability is, of course, very important.

128560.explanation: Facilitation is allowed but leave the organization to private initiatives. That is not a municipal task.

128561.explanation: There is nothing against sports activities, but this is not a municipal responsibility.

128562.explanation: Let promotion be done by parties who directly benefit from it. That is not a municipal task.

128563.explanation: Sometimes yes, sometimes no. Each time, the benefit for the city and its residents must be assessed.

128564.explanation: Freedom is important. Those who do not want to fly should not have to. Stop patronizing society and the residents of The Hague.

128565.explanation: The city's economy benefits from good entrepreneurs and companies, and the municipality must facilitate that optimally.

128566.explanation: Scheveningen and Dutch culture are of great importance.

128567.explanation: Much more efficient working is possible and necessary; this concerns taxpayers' money and it must be used as well as possible.

128568.explanation: There are too many people roaming around who do not belong in The Hague. The Hague is the most densely populated city in the Netherlands, so as far as the PVV is concerned, no asylum seeker is to enter The Hague anymore.

128569.explanation: That should be up to each entrepreneur. The Netherlands is a free country where you should be in control of your own money and energy.

128570.explanation: The responsibility lies with the entrepreneur and citizen themselves. In case of nuisance, the municipality does have a task.

128571.explanation: Given the scarcity of homes, the municipality should be happy with every house that goes for rent.

128572.explanation: In principle, the accessibility of the city is the responsibility of the municipality.

128573.explanation: The key here is AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE. If it can be done, then of course, but in consultation with residents and entrepreneurs to prevent undesirable consequences.

128574.explanation: This must be looked at on a case-by-case basis. History is very important, but it cannot simply go at the expense of mobility and traffic flow.

128575.explanation: Zero emission zones must be abolished as soon as possible.

128576.explanation: Provided it is done carefully and without animal suffering.

128577.explanation: This should be determined by the libraries and universities in the city themselves.

128578.explanation: Fewer foreign students is an important step so that the available rooms can be used by Dutch students.

128579.explanation: The education results are already substandard, so staying home must be counteracted.

128580.explanation: The municipality should not use citizens' tax money for others' hobbies. There must, however, be funding for libraries and museums.

128581.explanation: Certainly yes if the need can be demonstrated.

128582.explanation: Street design can certainly contribute to more safety but also pay attention to the causes of insecurity such as mass immigration.

128583.explanation: Good regulation and combating excesses is logical but that does not mean a whole industry needs to be abolished.

128584.explanation: Dutch culture and Scheveningen traditions are very important to the PVV.

128585.explanation: That should include extra training and education, but proper enforcement must be guaranteed.

128586.explanation: Residents increasingly complain about nuisance caused by drug properties. Therefore, closing and banning coffeeshops and shisha lounges is obvious.

128587.explanation: The PVV is not against new hotels by default. Good citizen participation is needed per case to prevent residents from experiencing nuisance.

128588.explanation: Property tax (ozb) should actually be lowered. The municipality must learn to manage money better and waste less so local taxes can go down.

128589.explanation: Status holders should come to The Hague as little as possible, and if they do, they can sign up at the bottom of the waiting list just like any Dutch person.

128590.explanation: This should be assessed case by case. Sometimes houses are for sale, sometimes they are un-rentable. But deliberate vacancy must obviously be tackled.

128591.explanation: Native roofless and homeless people must, of course, be helped and given priority. The normal urgency arrangement can be used for this.

128592.explanation: Urgency can be established in consultation, but there are also other important professional groups, so the PVV prefers individual solutions over such generic measures.

128593.explanation: Residents of The Hague should be able to get priority to continue living in their own neighborhood.

128594.explanation: Illegal is not lawful and enforcement is therefore necessary.

128595.explanation: This affair is resolved by the national government and it should be prevented that differences arise depending on the victim’s place of residence. It is better to arrange this well nationwide.

128596.explanation: Social assistance fraud is abuse of tax money and must always be combated.

128597.explanation: Where possible, it can be allowed but preferably not in shopping streets as that causes nuisance for pedestrians almost everywhere. Better at the end or beginning of shopping areas. Cyclists can also walk.

128598.explanation: In some cases it may be necessary but not to introduce it everywhere indiscriminately. Traffic in The Hague is much safer than in the rest of the Netherlands, so let’s not panic. Sometimes 30 km/h actually increases the chance of accidents.

128599.explanation: The harassment of cars must stop. The car is not a luxury but a necessity. Preserving more green space by less densification of the city is preferred.

128600.explanation: For some routes this might be necessary but again each case should first be investigated for usefulness and necessity.

128601.explanation: Prefer lower property tax and more purchasing power for citizens rather than these kinds of fixes.

128602.explanation: Unnecessary noise pollution must be combated.

128603.explanation: Let residents decide by referendum whether they agree with it.

128604.explanation: This is stated too generically. Sometimes yes, sometimes no. Should be considered per individual. It concerns tax money.

128605.explanation: The municipality must set priorities so more money becomes available for this care.

128606.explanation: The PVV supports smoke-free school grounds and good education for young people. Smoking should be forbidden for persons under 18.

128607.explanation: The PVV wants to prevent people from getting into debt by drastically lowering taxes and healthcare costs. Help should be given when truly needed, but attention must also remain for “small” creditors.

128608.explanation: The PVV wants to tighten access based on income and actual disabilities but this must be done with common sense and respect and without a bureaucratic maze.

128609.explanation: Healthcare and education must always be well accessible and remain so.

128610.explanation: It is absurd to allow 100,000 extra inhabitants to come to the city and then close hospitals. So Bronovo must remain open and become a full-fledged hospital again.

128611.explanation: The employer and the city where the company is located are responsible for the housing of migrant workers. Let Westland arrange this itself and not pass it on to The Hague.

128612.explanation: The PVV thinks that is a good idea.

128613.explanation: The PVV wants all new social housing to be allocated exclusively to residents of The Hague or starters from The Hague.

128614.explanation: The PVV opposes a ban on angling.

128615.explanation: What is green must remain green.

128616.explanation: Youth work that genuinely improves safety in the city has the full support of the PVV, but ideological or “woke” projects for youth are definitely not supported, so those could be cut.

128556.explanation: It is important that everyone understands the information from the municipality. In addition, each letter and email should mention a phone number and email address of a municipal contact person for questions.

128557.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes residents should be able to participate in decisions. That’s why we ensure good participation processes in decisions and a strong local democracy. This does not necessarily require expensive citizens’ assemblies.

128558.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP considers sports very important. We therefore like to invest in amateur sports that everyone can participate in.

128559.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP finds it important that every child can play safely in the neighborhood. This means we want to invest in enough playgrounds where children with disabilities can also play.

128560.explanation: Everyone must be able to play sports in The Hague, regardless of the size of their wallet. The ChristenUnie/SGP therefore invests in amateur sports and clubs, but does not consider it the primary task of the municipality to organize more itself.

128561.explanation: Organizing top sports events is not a priority for the ChristenUnie/SGP. First the basics must be in order.

128562.explanation: A city like The Hague sells itself. The ChristenUnie/SGP prefers to use that money for, for example, combating poverty or reducing homelessness.

128563.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes the congress function is important for The Hague and its position as an international City of Peace and Justice. We must maintain that position. Therefore, we will not cut this facility.

128564.explanation: This ban was introduced last year. The ChristenUnie/SGP believes we must be a reliable government and therefore should not reverse it.

128565.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP thinks it is important that entrepreneurs can work well in The Hague. They should not be bothered by municipal bureaucracy. The municipality must ensure entrepreneurs quickly get a permit if they meet the conditions.

128566.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP is proud of the Scheveningen Harbor and fishing industry. The municipality must do everything to preserve fishing for Scheveningen. This includes promoting fishing in Scheveningen.

128567.explanation: Reducing the number of municipal civil servants is not an end goal for the ChristenUnie/SGP. However, we want less expensive external hiring. The number of civil servants must align with municipal priorities and it is important they can work in a safe environment.

128568.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP finds it important to meet the national requirement to accommodate asylum seekers. We look for suitable, small-scale locations where asylum seekers can be housed.

128569.explanation: We encourage companies to save energy and do so ourselves at municipal buildings. It is already monitored, and as far as the ChristenUnie/SGP is concerned, there is no reason for stricter enforcement.

128570.explanation: Gambling can lead to addiction and serious financial problems. Especially young people and vulnerable residents are victims. Solving these problems costs the municipality a lot of money. The ChristenUnie/SGP therefore believes casinos and gambling halls in The Hague should be restricted.

128571.explanation: Everyone in The Hague should be able to live in a warm and comfortable home. By revoking landlord permits, the municipality should be able to compel landlords to make their homes more sustainable in the future.

128572.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes it is important that everyone in The Hague can participate. This means that the beach must be accessible to everyone.

128573.explanation: Garbage bags on the street cause nuisance, pollution, and attract pests. Therefore, the ChristenUnie/SGP wants to place underground containers where possible. Suitable locations must be carefully considered to avoid odor and noise nuisance for residents.

128574.explanation: It is not a priority for the ChristenUnie/SGP to let water flow through filled-in canals. The ChristenUnie/SGP first invests in neighborhoods with little green space and where residents suffer greatly from heat stress. Only when the basics are in order can canals be reopened.

128575.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP was not in favor of introducing a zero-emission zone along the coast in the short term. Now that a majority of the municipal council has decided otherwise, the ChristenUnie/SGP believes the municipality must be a reliable partner to entrepreneurs and not interfere with this.

128576.explanation: Seagulls can cause waste nuisance by opening garbage bags. Where this is a major problem, seagull nests should be able to be relocated with respect for the animals.

128577.explanation: There are already some places where people can study in the evenings and on weekends, but there could be more. More and more students are coming to our city, so more study spaces are urgently needed.

128578.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes it is important to have more student houses with shared kitchens and living rooms, paying attention to neighborhood livability. These homes reduce the shortage of student housing and loneliness among students.

128579.explanation: Every child should have access to appropriate education, within both regular and special education.

128580.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes it is important that there is sufficient cultural offering in the neighborhood. The cultural anchors in the city districts are important for this. The ChristenUnie/SGP wants a full-fledged cultural anchor in every city district.

128581.explanation: Every neighborhood should have at least one library. Therefore, more new libraries should be opened, such as in Mariahoeve.

128582.explanation: It is important that everyone can safely walk the streets. The ChristenUnie/SGP therefore values creating an overview of unsafe places in the city. These places should be addressed as a priority.

128583.explanation: Every person is valuable to the ChristenUnie/SGP, which is why prostitution and human trafficking do not belong in our city. The ChristenUnie/SGP wants to phase out window prostitution and ensure that prostitutes who stop receive good assistance and guidance.

128584.explanation: The bonfires have a long tradition in The Hague and Scheveningen and contribute to community spirit in the city. The ChristenUnie/SGP therefore believes bonfires should be allowed, provided they are safe.

128585.explanation: In principle, only the police may use force weapons. If the enforcement organization requests more means, we will investigate the necessary training and appropriate resources.

128586.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP wants an extinction policy for coffee shops: if a coffee shop owner stops, the business disappears and no new one replaces it.

128587.explanation: There are enough hotels in The Hague. The ChristenUnie/SGP believes no new hotels are needed in the city. Instead, we want to invest in sufficient housing for people who are currently homeless or without a home.

128588.explanation: For the ChristenUnie/SGP, increasing taxes such as property tax (ozb) is not a goal in itself.

128589.explanation: Those who need it most should be prioritized. The ChristenUnie/SGP acknowledges the task is large and the waiting list is long. Therefore, we want to invest extra in building more social rental housing.

128590.explanation: The housing shortage in The Hague is significant. The ChristenUnie/SGP believes we must seize every opportunity to tackle the housing shortage. A vacancy ordinance can reduce the number of empty buildings.

128591.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes it is important that everyone has a roof over their head. We believe in the 'housing first' principle. By giving priority in certain cases to homeless people, we can reduce pressure on the scarce places in social shelters.

128592.explanation: Teachers, nurses, and police officers are indispensable in our city and we have a shortage. The ChristenUnie/SGP therefore wants to prioritize them when allocating some social and especially mid-priced rental homes.

128593.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes it is important that people who have lived long in a neighborhood can move within their own neighborhood. These residents are the social cement of the neighborhood. We wish young and old who wish to stay in their own neighborhood a nice place nearby.

128594.explanation: Illegal room occupancy causes nuisance and poorer livability. The ChristenUnie/SGP believes the Pandbrigade should inspect more strictly in neighborhoods with the biggest problems, such as Rustenburg-Oostbroek and Transvaal. We therefore also invest extra in expanding the Pandbrigade.

128595.explanation: The municipality must properly support victims of the benefits scandal; they receive help through the municipality. The national government provides compensation.

128596.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP believes the government should act from a position of trust. People receiving social assistance deserve a government that helps them as well as possible.

128597.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP thinks it is important to encourage residents to cycle to work and school. It is important that there are enough bike racks to park bicycles. The municipality should invest in this.

128598.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, safety comes first. A speed limit of 30 km/h is safe for children playing on the street. Road flow around neighborhoods is important, so not every street in the city should have a 30 km/h limit.

128599.explanation: Building parking spaces requires a tailored approach. In some places there are surplus parking spots that can be replaced by greenery; in other places parking spaces are scarce and a lot of greenery is already present.

128600.explanation: Building new tram lines is expensive, but the ChristenUnie/SGP wants to invest in good public transport and tunneling of tram lines. This keeps the city accessible in a green way and ready for the future.

128601.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP finds it important that everyone can travel easily in The Hague. Therefore, we want to make public transport cheaper for people living in poverty and free for children under 12 living in poverty.

128602.explanation: Noisy motorcycle engines have been a nuisance to residents for years. The ChristenUnie/SGP therefore believes that sound speed cameras should be deployed quickly, starting at the places with the most nuisance, such as Westduinweg.

128603.explanation: Paid parking should not be a cash cow. It may only be introduced if there is support for it in the neighborhood. Parking is free on Sundays and holidays where possible.

128604.explanation: For not everyone is care in kind the most appropriate form of care. If people want to purchase their own care, this should not be made unnecessarily difficult.

128605.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP finds it important that children grow up in a safe and healthy environment. We focus on as much preventive care as possible, so that those who really need help get it immediately.

128606.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP invests in a healthy generation. We discourage smoking and vaping by banning these activities around schools and playgrounds.

128607.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP thinks it is important that people receive help quickly and well. We fully commit to a debt-free life and aftercare following a trajectory.

128608.explanation: The municipality must monitor. We do this through (re)assessments, but there is no reason to do this more strictly than we currently do.

128609.explanation: The ChristenUnie/SGP only wants an income-dependent personal contribution.

128610.explanation: Due to staff shortages, it is necessary to concentrate some functions and expertise, such as emergency care. However, at least an outpatient clinic must be retained in Bronovo.

128611.explanation: The streets in The Hague, for example in Laak, are flooded with migrant workers who are brought here by unscrupulous employment agencies to work, among other places, in the Westland. Companies that bring migrant workers here must provide decent housing themselves.

128612.explanation: The Christian Union/SGP believes we must invest in a green city, especially in neighborhoods with little greenery where people suffer from heat stress.

128613.explanation: The Christian Union/SGP wants at least 30% of new-build homes to be social rental housing, and 50% to be mid-priced rental or affordable owner-occupied homes. Forcing 50% everywhere would stall housing development. Developers cannot realize that.

128614.explanation: Many residents of The Hague, Scheveningen, and Loosduinen love (sport) fishing. It goes too far to ban it.

128615.explanation: The municipal council of The Hague is responsible for policy within its city limits.

128616.explanation: Youth workers are indispensable in our city. The Christian Union/SGP believes we must invest in youth workers, because by doing so we invest in our youth. They are our future.

128556.explanation: FVD wants all letters and emails from the municipality to be available in clear Dutch.

128557.explanation: FVD wants municipal referenda that all residents of The Hague can participate in. No advisory body of randomly selected residents.

128558.explanation: Top athletes serve as role models for youth. It is important for young people to have local athletes they look up to and from whom they can learn discipline and a winning mentality.

128559.explanation: It is important that children have a place to gather and be active. However, whether new playgrounds are necessary must be evaluated per neighborhood.

128560.explanation: Top sports events are also an opportunity to promote amateur sports. It would be good to create space for amateurs around professional competitions.

128561.explanation: Large sporting events give a boost to the local economy and increase the city's name recognition.

128562.explanation: It is the municipality's task to shape a city that promotes itself without expensive marketing campaigns.

128563.explanation: It is good for The Hague to put itself on the map by facilitating such events.

128564.explanation: The ban on advertising products that use 'fossil fuels' such as air travel, cars, and boat trips is a ridiculous attempt to influence citizens' behavior.

128565.explanation: Applying for permits currently involves a mountain of paperwork. FVD wants to reduce bureaucracy and simplify the permit process. This can be done by cutting requirements related to 'diversity' and 'CO2 footprint'.

128566.explanation: The Hague's fishery is among the best in the country. Besides, locally caught fish always tastes best.

128567.explanation: Over 10,000 municipal employees consume more than a billion euros each year. This is excessive. With the help of AI technology, among others, it is possible to make administrative processes more efficient. A smaller municipality provides more space for its residents.

128568.explanation: Native Dutch are a minority of less than 40% in The Hague. As a result, illiteracy and street insecurity are increasing. FVD wants The Hague to become Dutch again. Therefore, the municipality must exempt itself from the dispersal law and close asylum seekers' centers.

128569.explanation: Energy is very expensive money that companies would rather spend elsewhere. It is therefore unnecessary and undesirable for the municipality to involve itself with companies' energy consumption.

128570.explanation: The municipality should be restrained in banning things. Entrepreneurs should have as much freedom as possible in their business operations, including in the gambling industry.

128571.explanation: Landlords and tenants can perfectly well judge on their own whether it is profitable to (re)rent a poorly insulated home.

128572.explanation: Accessible beaches are important so that all residents can enjoy the sea. However, accessibility measures should not come at the cost of the view.

128573.explanation: Where possible, waste should be disposed of in underground containers to reduce nuisance from seagulls and litter. Where this is not possible, garbage bags must be collected regularly.

128574.explanation: Letting water flow through the filled-in canals would be a mega project with very limited benefits. The Hague would benefit more from beautiful new construction and well-functioning infrastructure.

128575.explanation: The zero-emission zones are a disaster for residents and entrepreneurs. Entrepreneurs are forced into huge investments or simply avoid The Hague. As a result, residents can no longer find a handyman and groceries become more expensive.

128576.explanation: Quality of life is important. When residents suffer nuisance from seagull nests, these must be able to be relocated.

128577.explanation: Accessible study places in libraries are important so everyone can study in peace. However, this should not turn into places where young people hang out and cause nuisance.

128578.explanation: Landlords should be free to rent their homes to students; they contribute to the vibrancy of the city.

128579.explanation: For some students with special learning needs, it is better to receive home schooling than be in a classroom. This is also better for the class, where the teacher's attention would otherwise be unevenly distributed.

128580.explanation: Funding for art and culture should be spent on art that is beautiful and strengthens our sense of community. Not on the ugly projects we have seen in recent years.

128581.explanation: Libraries are important for the development of residents. Libraries do not solve illiteracy, but they do give eager residents more opportunities to grow and learn.

128582.explanation: Lighting can contribute to women's sense of safety, but it is not the cause of the problem. FVD wants stricter enforcement against troublemakers and closure of asylum seekers' centers with risk groups.

128583.explanation: Window prostitution is ugly and causes a lot of nuisance. Meanwhile, there are sufficient alternatives available.

128584.explanation: The bonfires are a beautiful tradition that must be cherished. FVD wants to provide full space for them.

128585.explanation: Insecurity in Scheveningen is a major problem. In recent years, residents and law enforcement have been terrorized by rioters. BOAs should also be armed to counter this.

128586.explanation: Coffeeshops encourage crime, cause odor nuisance, and harm the streetscape.

128587.explanation: Hotels contribute to tourism and the local economy.

128588.explanation: Due to senseless projects and bureaucratic excesses, municipal spending keeps growing. Citizens pay the bill for this. The municipality must cut costs and lower taxes.

128589.explanation: Status holders are not Dutch. They have not contributed to building our country and its facilities. As far as FVD is concerned, Dutch nationals should be prioritized over status holders, not the other way around.

128590.explanation: Homeowners are free to use their homes as they wish. The housing shortage must be solved by reversing mass immigration and building homes, not by imposing additional taxes.

128591.explanation: The Hague is the most densely populated city in the Netherlands. There are many (foreign) homeless people in The Hague. It is better to accommodate them outside the city (or the country).

128592.explanation: Teachers, nurses, and police officers play an essential role in the functioning of neighborhoods. Therefore, FVD supports a priority scheme for social rental housing in these specific cases.

128593.explanation: It is important that citizens can find a home in the neighborhood where they are rooted. This protects the social fabric.

128594.explanation: Illegal room occupancy burdens infrastructure without contributing to it. FVD wants stricter enforcement against illegal rentals and occupancy.

128595.explanation: The tax authorities are mainly responsible for the childcare benefits scandal. This is a responsibility of the national government. It is not intended that the municipality should take the blame for a problem it did not cause.

128596.explanation: The social benefits and subsidy system has completely gotten out of hand. It discourages working, consumes a tremendous amount of taxpayer money, and should therefore be limited.

128597.explanation: FVD supports large underground bicycle garages but does not want bike racks to be used to limit accessibility for motorists.

128598.explanation: FVD wants The Hague to remain easily accessible. A maximum speed of 30 km/h causes less flow and irritation.

128599.explanation: Parking spaces are essential for citizens and entrepreneurs to reach the city. FVD wants space for motorists so The Hague remains mobile.

128600.explanation: The municipality can build new tram lines where possible and necessary. In many cases, bus connections are preferred because they require less investment and are more flexible.

128601.explanation: Affordable public transport is important for a mobile, accessible, and livable The Hague.

128602.explanation: Traffic offenders who terrorize the city with their rented Mercedes must be dealt with severely. In some places, such as the Scheveningen boulevard, noise cameras can be installed. The privacy of citizens must be safeguarded in this.

128603.explanation: FVD is a strong supporter of direct democracy. Referendums are the most effective way to involve citizens in the future of their city.

128604.explanation: FVD believes in a The Hague where the community supports each other. Not everything needs to be solved by the government.

128605.explanation: Currently, youth care is used far too much. Children must also learn to stand up for themselves, and not every setback is the responsibility of doctors or coaches. Necessary youth care must remain available for young people who truly need it.

128606.explanation: FVD does not want the municipality to take the role of parents or schools. Schools can decide for themselves about smoking areas. Instituting smoking bans is an unacceptable infringement on personal life.

128607.explanation: When a resident has debts, it is in the interest of their environment, and therefore of the municipality, to help them get financially back on track. Not by paying off their debt, but by fulfilling an advisory role.

128608.explanation: Home care can assess on its own if a client needs cleaning assistance.

128609.explanation: Public transport costs money, as does the care taxi. Those who use it must pay a small personal contribution.

128610.explanation: Benoordenhout is a neighborhood with great care needs. By fully utilizing the Bronovo Hospital, residents need to travel less far for their care.

128611.explanation: If an entrepreneur wants to use cheap foreign labor, they must bear the costs themselves and not pass them to the municipality.

128612.explanation: FVD strongly supports a green inner city. However, this proposal ultimately leads to an exponential increase in trees in the city. Green spaces require a tailored approach and must be considered on a neighborhood-by-neighborhood basis.

128613.explanation: More social rental housing means fewer homes in the private sector. That means higher rents. FVD wants less social rental housing so private rents become affordable again.

128614.explanation: Fishing is a relaxing pastime for many residents that also maintains social contacts. FVD recognizes the importance of this and does not want to take this enjoyable activity away from citizens.

128615.explanation: The Hague is the most densely populated city in the Netherlands. Preserving uninhabited areas is essential for livability.

128616.explanation: It is the role of parents to guide their child in growing up, not that of the municipality.

128556.explanation: Many people in The Hague have difficulty reading and writing. Volt finds it important that the municipality approaches you in a way that suits you.

128557.explanation: People must be well involved in the decisions the municipality makes. Volt wants an annual citywide citizens' assembly with a follow-up obligation for the municipal council. Additionally, the municipality must hold citizens' assemblies annually in each district.

128558.explanation: Volt advocates for a broad, safe, and inclusive sports environment where everyone is encouraged to participate, including people with mental or physical disabilities. Top-level sports and their events are a good way to inspire young people and put The Hague on the map.

128559.explanation: Play areas contribute to children's health and well-being and to livability. Volt believes these should be located at a safe distance from traffic and constructed sustainably.

128560.explanation: Playing sports is good for your physical and mental health and brings people together. Volt believes sports activities should be as inclusive as possible.

128561.explanation: Sports events bring residents together, provide opportunities for entrepreneurs, and strengthen The Hague’s identity. Volt wants to develop an events vision together with The Hague residents that preserves and renews existing events while adding new initiatives.

128562.explanation: As the City of Peace and Justice with one of the few major European cities having a beach, The Hague has unique opportunities to attract entrepreneurs and tourists. Volt therefore wants to strengthen the promotion and visibility of The Hague.

128563.explanation: Volt wants to strengthen The Hague's international character. By properly housing international organizations and attracting new ones, The Hague enhances its global position, creates jobs, and attracts conferences. Structural facilities around the World Forum reduce nuisance.

128564.explanation: Volt wants to move towards a sustainable society. Advertisements for air travel vacations and diesel cars do not fit that society. It is good that the municipality has already begun working on this.

128565.explanation: Volt advocates for easy entrepreneurship. We bundle all information, regulations, processes for permits, and contact points into one recognizable and visible business portal. These processes become faster, clearer, and more transparent, so entrepreneurs know exactly where they stand.

128566.explanation: Volt wants to invest in existing sectors in the city by making them safe, sustainable, and future-proof. This also applies to port-related activities such as fishing and offshore industries. If it helps to occasionally serve North Sea products at promotional events, that is acceptable.

128567.explanation: Volt wants to move towards a smaller municipal organization that efficiently uses European AI and digitization.

128568.explanation: Less accommodation does not lead to fewer asylum seekers, but to crisis shelters, overcrowded centers, and inhumane situations. That helps no one. Volt believes the city should comply with the legal obligation from the Distribution Act to provide good, small-scale, and humane accommodation for asylum seekers.

128569.explanation: For Volt, sustainability is very important. More monitoring creates unnecessary administrative burdens. The municipality should focus on the parties that are not yet so advanced and can collaborate much better with the business community, for example by procuring as circularly as possible.

128570.explanation: There are more and more people dealing with gambling addiction. Compared to online gambling, casinos and betting halls have supervision.

128571.explanation: For Volt, sustainability is very important. Banning causes a greater shortage of housing. Volt prefers that rules that make greening houses and buildings difficult be removed. The municipality must also ensure fewer obstacles to implementing insulation.

128572.explanation: Volt wants the Hague beach to be sustainable, lively, and accessible, with attention to protecting nature. In addition to ramps, for example, the public toilets at the beach should also be wheelchair accessible.

128573.explanation: Volt chooses a street litter approach that suits the environment. Underground containers can help improve livability and the streetscape. It is also important to consider the surroundings and how to help people manage their waste properly.

128574.explanation: Volt wants to restore the canals to their former glory. Green and water cool and strengthen each other, especially during hot summers. The Hague Creek should flow through the entire city again, so we can enjoy our city from the water.

128575.explanation: Volt wants the city to become cleaner and healthier by maintaining the zero-emission zones. We also consider reversing this a bad signal to all entrepreneurs along the coast who have invested in expensive electric vehicles over the past year.

128576.explanation: Seagulls cause nuisance in The Hague, and relocating seagull nests could be a possible solution if done properly. However, this is currently not allowed under national legislation protecting the birds.

128577.explanation: Extending library opening hours ensures that students and others can make even better use of these community services.

128578.explanation: Volt believes a good home is the foundation for a pleasant student life. Together with housing corporations, we focus on building enough student housing: studios and houses with space for meeting and co-living. On the private market, rules for room rentals become more flexible under appropriate conditions.

128579.explanation: Volt wants every child to have the opportunity to develop at school. When children stay home, they miss out on education, social contacts, and growth opportunities. The truancy officer will therefore have more room for tailor-made solutions and direct contact with the child.

128580.explanation: Volt sees art and culture as one of the foundations on which we bring people together, develop talents, and support the local economy. Therefore, the art and cultural offerings should be given room to grow.

128581.explanation: Libraries are places where people meet and share their creativity. Moreover, libraries are used for language lessons or help with digital accessibility. Volt believes everyone should have a library nearby.
