128583.explanation: Regulation and supervision provide more safety than displacement. Closure often leads to invisible and more unsafe situations for sex workers and the surrounding area.

128584.explanation: Traditions are important, but safety, health, and community support must always come first. Only under strict conditions and proper organization.

128585.explanation: Enforcement officers often work in tense situations and deserve good protection. Extra resources should contribute to their safety and to peace on the streets.

128586.explanation: Reducing coffeeshops does not automatically lead to less nuisance or use. Good regulation, distribution, and enforcement provide more safety and control than closures.

128587.explanation: Tourism and business visitors contribute to The Hague’s economy and employment. Growth must be balanced with livability for residents.

128588.explanation: Raising property tax mainly affects people with fixed or low incomes, including many elderly. 50PLUS chooses cost control and efficient use of municipal funds.

128589.explanation: Housing shortage affects many groups. Priority based on urgency and length of residence is fairer and increases support among housing seekers.

128590.explanation: Vacancy is undesirable in times of housing shortage. But an extra incentive will not help make homes available to residents any faster.

128591.explanation: A stable place to live is essential to offer people perspective. Housing is the basis for recovery and social participation. Nevertheless, 50PLUS believes granting priority contributes nothing.

128592.explanation: These professions are indispensable for the city. Good housing helps retain staff and limit shortages. Nevertheless, 50PLUS believes granting priority contributes nothing.

128593.explanation: This promotes social cohesion and prevents people from having to leave their familiar environment.

128594.explanation: Illegal room rental leads to unsafe situations and nuisance. Strict enforcement protects residents and neighborhoods.

128595.explanation: The government has seriously failed people. Municipalities must contribute to recovery, support, and trust.

128596.explanation: Abuse undermines support for social services. At the same time, tailored assistance remains necessary for those who truly need help.

128597.explanation: Good bicycle facilities encourage sustainable transport and reduce annoyingly parked bikes.

128598.explanation: Lower speed increases traffic safety and livability, especially for the elderly and children.

128599.explanation: Green space is important, but accessibility must be maintained. Mainly elderly and less mobile residents depend on the car.

128600.explanation: Public transport is important, but new lines must have demonstrable added value. Costs and nuisance must be carefully weighed.

128601.explanation: Free public transport for children lowers the threshold for families to use public transit. It promotes sustainable mobility, relieves parents financially, and helps children become familiar with public transport from a young age.

128602.explanation: Traffic noise has proven effects on health and residential enjoyment, especially for the elderly. Extra enforcement, for example with sound measurements, contributes to quieter and more livable neighborhoods.

128603.explanation: Introducing paid parking has major consequences for residents. 50PLUS believes residents should have a say so that support and customization per neighborhood are possible.

128604.explanation: A personal budget gives people control over their own care. Faster and less bureaucratic allocation prevents stress and ensures timely appropriate help.

128605.explanation: Children and families must receive timely help. Early support prevents more serious problems later and is both humane and socially responsible.

128606.explanation: Children must be protected from harmful influences and bad examples. A smoke- and vape-free environment contributes to health and prevention.

128607.explanation: Debts can quickly spiral out of control and have major consequences for health and family situations. Early and accessible help prevents long-term poverty and societal costs.

128608.explanation: Municipal care must reach those who truly need it. Extra checks can be done afterward when the municipality deems it necessary. There should be trust instead of suspicion.

128609.explanation: Transport to care or education must not become a financial barrier. Necessary mobility must remain accessible for vulnerable residents.

128610.explanation: Accessible care close to home is crucial, especially for the elderly. A full hospital with emergency care and outpatient clinics strengthens care security in The Hague.

128611.explanation: This prevents extra pressure on the housing market and nuisance in residential neighborhoods. Employers bear responsibility for decent housing of their staff.

128612.explanation: Green space is essential for livability, health, and climate adaptation.

128613.explanation: The housing market calls for balance. Too high fixed percentages limit flow and diversity; tailoring per location works better.

128614.explanation: Angling is an allowed and popular form of recreation, especially among the elderly. Well regulated, it poses no problem for nature or surroundings.

128615.explanation: Building within the city is preferred but should not become a dogma. In exceptional cases, expansion may be necessary to solve housing shortages.

128616.explanation: Youth work prevents problems such as nuisance, school dropout, and crime. Investing in youth is investing in the future and saves higher costs later.

statements.title: The municipality should permanently ban advertising posters for flight holidays and diesel cars.
statements.moreInfo: Since January 1, 2025 it has been forbidden in The Hague to advertise polluting services and products outdoors that are harmful to the climate. Permanently banning this means enforcing the ban on fossil-fuel outdoor advertising.

The number of coffeeshops in The Hague should be reduced.

The municipality should allow the relocation of seagull nests to reduce nuisance.
The municipality should establish a citizens' assembly.

statements.title: Trams and buses in The Hague should remain free for children up to 12 years old.

The municipality should ensure that water flows again through previously filled-in canals.
Polluting delivery vans and trucks should be allowed to drive along the coast and in the city center again.
statements.moreInfo: Traffic in The Hague worsens air quality. The municipality has therefore introduced environmental zones along the coast and in the city center. Diesel vehicles that cause extra air pollution are no longer allowed there.

The municipality should combat window prostitution.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should give neighborhood residents priority for a home as soon as one becomes available in their neighborhood.
The municipality should more strictly check whether home-care recipients actually need cleaning assistance.
The speed limit in all residential neighborhoods of The Hague should be 30 km/h.
The municipality should give priority to homeless people when allocating social housing.
The number of municipal employees should be reduced.
The municipality should continue to give priority to status holders when allocating social housing.
The municipality should ban casinos and gambling halls from being open 24 hours a day.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside the existing city limits.
The municipality should organize more sports activities that all residents can participate in.
The municipality should provide extra support to the victims of the childcare benefits scandal.
From 2030 the municipality should be able to forbid landlords from renting out poorly insulated homes.
The municipality should cut spending on facilities for large conferences and international meetings.
The municipality should promote fishing in Scheveningen, for example by serving North Sea fish at its own events.
The municipality should raise the property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB).
The municipality should replace street rubbish bags as much as possible with underground containers.
The municipality should more strictly enforce rules against illegal room occupancy.
The municipality should not spend money on promoting elite sports.
The municipality should create new play areas in every neighborhood.
The municipality should more strictly check whether companies are saving enough energy.
The municipality should only send letters and emails that are understandable to everyone.
The municipality should make all beach access points accessible to everyone, for example by providing ramps for wheelchairs.
To make women feel safer on the street, the municipality should invest more in street lighting and pruning.
The municipality should ensure that by 2030 there is no longer a waiting list for youth care.
The municipality should open accessible study spaces in libraries and other public places in the evenings and on weekends.
The municipality should strive to ensure that no child misses school.
The municipality should spend more money on art and culture.
The Hague should open new libraries.
The municipality should build new tram lines.
The municipality must increase enforcement of traffic noise, for example with noise cameras.
The municipality should make it easier and faster for citizens to receive a personal budget (pgb).
The municipality should give people with debts easier and faster access to debt counseling.
